Holding 9♥4♦ in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 (SB folded). This is a very weak, offsuit hand that is largely dominated by a reasonable BTN open range. Calling 200 to see a flop leaves me out of position and frequently in multi-street, low-equity spots — the pot odds (~30.8%) don't make up for my likely equity disadvantage. A minimum reraise to 500 would be a thin bluff that will often be called or repopped; I don't want to bloat the pot OOP with junk. With a healthy stack and plenty of hands left, it's better to preserve chips and fold.

Preflop on the button with 4c Th, a weak offsuit gapped hand with poor playability and low equity against a likely range from the raiser (to call 100 into 150 pot suggests a standard raise from SB or BB). Pot odds require roughly 40% equity (100/250), but T4o has far less against typical raising ranges. Deep stacks, but no reason to speculate with trash; fold to preserve stack for better opportunities. Opponents aggressive per history, but this spot is a clear fold.

Raise to 200 (total). Q♣8♣ is a playable, suited queen worth opening heads-up from the small blind — it has good equity and plays well postflop. Limping/calling 50 is too passive and gives the big blind a free look; a 200 raise takes the initiative, narrows Flash’s calling range, and preserves pot control with my 5300 stack. If Flash 3-bets large or jams I’ll fold; versus a small 3-bet I can call or flat to play postflop and leverage implied odds and suitedness.

I am in the Big Blind holding K8o. Minni raised to 200 from the Small Blind, which is a small min-raise. I need to call 100 more into a pot of 300, giving me 3:1 odds. K8o is a strong enough hand to defend in a blind-versus-blind situation against a wide range, even though I'm out of position. However, since the stacks are relatively shallow (around 50BB) and I am closing the action pre-flop, calling is the most standard and low-risk play here.

I raised preflop with Qc8c to take the initiative and narrow Flash’s calling range. The flop A♠-6♦-5♥ is a bad one for my hand: I have no pair, only queen-high, and only backdoor club/very remote straight outs. Against a single opponent who called a raise from the big blind, a continuation bet is standard to leverage my preflop initiative and pick off hands like Kx, Qx, small pocket pairs and many floats. I’ll use a half‑pot c-bet (200) — large enough to get some folds and protect against free cards, but small enough to keep the pot manageable if called. Plan if raised: fold to significant flop aggression (I have very little showdown equity vs A or a set). If called, proceed cautiously on the turn and look to improve (pair or club) before committing more chips.

On an Ace-high board, the Small Blind's pre-flop raising range is very strong, and a 1/3 pot continuation bet is standard for many players with their entire range. However, with King-high and no real draws (only running straight or pair possibilities), I have very little equity against a range that includes many Aces and better pairs. Floating out of position here is generally a losing play, so I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I’m on the button with K♣J♠ — a strong, positionally advantaged hand with ~55BB effective. I want to open for value and to seize initiative, so I’ll raise rather than just call. A standard 3x BB to 300 is consistent with the table and gives fold equity while keeping a pot I can play post-flop in position. After my raise the pot will be ~450 and opponents must pay to see the flop (Flash must add more from the SB, Elon the BB). Plan: if I face a small/standard 3-bet I’ll usually call and use position to play the flop; against a large 3-bet (near pot) I’ll fold KJ offsuit unless I have a specific read. If called, I’ll c-bet favorable textures and control the pot on bad boards.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Five offsuit. Minni, on the button, has raised to 300. While A5o is a marginal hand, Minni has been very active with button raises. However, looking at the stack sizes, both Minni and Elon are quite short (around 50-60 big blinds). In a three-handed game, A5o is often a call or a fold from the small blind against a button raise, but given the shallow stacks and Minni's frequency, I could 3-bet. However, calling is safer to see a flop and control the pot size with a hand that has good top-pair potential but weak kicker issues. Given the specific dynamics and the need to maximize the stack over 150 hands, I will call the 250 to see a flop.

86o is a trash hand with no straight or flush potential. Facing a button raise from Minni and a call from Flash in SB, both likely have decent ranges. Pot odds of calling 200 into 900 (~18% needed) but equity is much lower against two players. Short stack at ~56BB, better to wait for better spots. Standard fold.

On this dry, rainbow flop of Qh-7c-2h, my Ace-high has some showdown value but is vulnerable. Minni, who raised pre-flop, will likely continuation bet many of her holdings. I am checking to her to see how she responds. If she checks behind, my hand is often the best. If she bets, I can evaluate based on the sizing, as I have two overcards and backdoor straight/flush possibilities (though very weak). Checking is the standard play for the out-of-position caller on this board texture.

I raised preflop from the button with K♣J♠ and the flop came 2♥ Q♥ 7♣. I have no pair and no meaningful draw—just king-high—so this is a pure bluff opportunity. As the preflop aggressor and last to act, a standard c-bet puts pressure on the SB’s wide calling range and folds out many A-high and marginal hands on this fairly dry board. I’ll size to about half the pot to balance fold equity and pot control; if I face significant aggression I’ll fold KJ (poor showdown value). If called I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and re-evaluate. Bet 350 into 700.

On a dry flop of Q-7-2, Ace-high has some showdown value, but playing it out of position against Minni's continuation bet is difficult. I have no significant draws or backdoor equity with Ad-5s. While I might have the best hand if she is bluffing with two undercards, calling here often leads to difficult decisions on the turn and river. Given my significant stack lead and the shallow stacks of my opponents, I prefer to fold this marginal hand and wait for a better opportunity where I have more equity or positional advantage.

I am on the button with 9s 6c. This is a weak hand to open with, especially since the small and big blinds (Elon and Minni) have been playing fairly aggressively in previous hands. With a massive chip lead, I don't need to take unnecessary risks with marginal holdings from the button. Folding is the safest and most standard play here.

Preflop in the small blind, folded to me with Jd3h, a complete trash hand with no connectivity, suitedness, or playability. Heads-up against Minni in the big blind, who has shown aggression in position but folding here saves chips for better spots. Completing the blind with this hand has negative EV long-term, as it plays poorly postflop. Standard fold to preserve stack.

3-handed on the button first to act preflop with 4c5c, a suited connector. Excellent position to open wide. Standard raise to 3x BB (300) to steal blinds, build pot, or play postflop multiway. Hand has good playability, implied odds with deep stacks (especially Flash). Folding too tight UTG 3-handed; limping passive and invites multiway pots OOP. Raise takes initiative.

I’m in the small blind (SB) with Td6d facing a Button open to 300. Pot is 450 and I must call 250 to continue (stacks ~59–60bb). T6s is a weak, often‑dominated hand that plays poorly out of position; calling here simply to see a flop invites a difficult OOP multiway pot (and the big blind still has to act). A light 3‑bet to the minimum (500) is too thin — it won’t give reliable fold equity vs a BTN open and leaves me OOP versus a wide calling/3‑bet range. Preserve my stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

I am in the Big Blind with T8o. Elon, on the button, has raised to 300 (3x the big blind). Minni has folded. I need to call 200 more into a pot that will be 450. Although T8o is a marginal hand, getting better than 2:1 odds to defend my big blind against a button open is generally acceptable in a 3-handed game, especially with the current stack depths. I will call to see a flop and use my stack advantage if necessary.

I have top pair (Tens) on a paired board of Tc-7c-7s. While my kicker (8) is mediocre, my hand is currently very strong against a button opening range. In this situation, I will follow standard procedure and check to the pre-flop aggressor. Leading out (donk betting) would likely force out his bluffs and only get called by hands that beat me or have significant equity. By checking, I allow Elon to continuation-bet with his air or draws, and I can then decide whether to call or raise based on his sizing.

Flop is Tc 7c 7s, paired board with two clubs. I hold 4c5c, giving me the nut flush draw (four clubs to the Tc flush) and good position. Flash called my button raise OOP from BB and checked, representing possible weak pairs, Ax, or bluffs. The paired board reduces some straight draws but my flush draw is strong. With initiative and position, c-betting half pot (~325, sizing to 400) builds the pot, charges Flash's draws or weak hands, denies equity, and takes advantage of fold equity on a dry-ish paired board. Checking back risks giving free cards to overcards or draws. Deep stacks allow for implied odds if I hit. Standard continuation bet here to maximize EV.

On a board of Tc-7c-7s, I have top pair (Tens) with an 8 kicker. Elon's button-opening range is quite wide, and a standard continuation bet of 400 into 650 is expected from many hands, including overcards (AK, AQ), flush draws, and smaller pocket pairs. Raising here would likely force out his bluffs while only being called by better hands (higher kickers, overpairs, or the unlikely 7x). By calling, I keep his range wide and allow him to continue bluffing on the turn, while also controlling the size of the pot with a hand that is strong but not invincible.

On the turn, the 4 of diamonds is a complete brick that does not change the board texture significantly. I still have top pair (Tens) on a board with a pair of sevens. Being out of position against Elon, the button raiser, checking remains the most prudent play. It allows Elon to continue bluffing with his air or semi-bluffing with flush draws, which I can then comfortably call. Betting into him now might fold out his bluffs while keeping me in the pot against hands that have me beaten, such as a trip 7s or a higher pocket pair. I will check to see how he reacts to the turn card.

Turn board 4d is a blank for most ranges, pairing my 5-high kicker into bottom pair while preserving my nut club flush draw. Pot is 1450, Flash has checked again after calling flop c-bet, showing weakness or trapping with a 7. My range as button raiser includes many overpairs, better 7s, and draws, so I have good credibility. Betting ~2/3 pot (975 rounds to 1000) extracts value from worse pairs/draws (e.g., 8x, 6x, flush draws, straight draws), protects against overcards completing, and denies equity to floats/OESD. Checking risks giving free cards to villain's equity or facing a river bet out of position. Position allows me to control pot size; deep stacks (Flash covered) support implied odds if I hit. EV+ to bet given fold equity on paired board and initiative.

On the turn, the 4 of diamonds is a brick that doesn't change the board texture. I have top pair (Tens) on a board of Tc-7c-7s-4d. Elon, the button raiser, is continuing his aggression with a bet of 1,000 into a 1,450 pot. His range includes trip 7s, overpairs (JJ-AA), better Tens (AT, KT, QT, JT), flush draws, and some total air. My T8o is a decent bluff-catcher in this spot. While I am behind his value range, the pot odds (calling 1,000 to win 3,450 total) and the wide nature of button-opening ranges in a 3-handed game make a fold too conservative. Raising would likely only be called by better hands, so I will call to see the river and evaluate based on the final card and Elon's action.

On the river, the board is Tc-7c-7s-4d-9d. I have top pair (Tens) with an 8 kicker. My hand is a classic bluff-catcher in this spot. I have checked the flop and turn, calling Elon's bets. The river 9d is relatively safe, though it does connect some straight possibilities (8J). I will continue with my strategy of checking to the aggressor. If Elon bets again, I will have to evaluate based on his sizing, but for now, checking is the most appropriate action to avoid value-cutting myself against his stronger range while allowing him to bluff with missed draws like the club flush.

River 9d is a blankish card that doesn't change much but pairs the board further with possible straight outs limited. My hand improved to bottom pair on turn but remains weak against Flash's calling range OOP through two streets (preflop raise call, flop cbet call into paired board, large turn bet call). Flash's range is strong: likely pocket 7s for trips (huge), overpairs like JJ+, T7s/Tc, 7x combos (e.g., 78, A7), or stubborn Ax/club draws. Nut flush draw missed, and I have no showdown value against better pairs/sets. Flash checks again OOP, which could be trapping monsters or giving up weaker hands. Betting risks getting raised off equity or called by better; thin value from pure air unlikely given board texture and villain's tenacity. Fold equity diminishes after two calls. Checking preserves stack, induces bluffs/showdowns with worse (missed draws/OESD like 56, 89), and realizes equity passively. Position allows free showdown with a marginal hand that blocks some villain value. Long-term EV maximized by not overbluffing sticky opponent in multi-street spot.

I’m on the button with K♦Q♠ in a 3-handed pot — this is a standard open-raise spot. Pot is 150 (50/100 blinds) and the minimum raise is 200, but I want meaningful fold equity against both blinds and to isolate the shorter big blind (Elon, ~38bb) while retaining post‑flop initiative. Flash (SB) is deep and has shown aggression previously, so a 3x open is large enough to discourage limp/calls from very weak hands but not overly commit the stack if I face heavy resistance. Plan: open to 300 total; if called I’ll use position to continuation-bet favorable flops and realize equity; fold to an over-aggressive-sized 3-bet from Flash unless the price/pot odds justify a call, and call a BB shove depending on his shove range and pot odds.

In Hand #45, I am in the Small Blind holding Queen-Trey of clubs. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. This hand is quite weak and generally does not play well out of position against a range that opens from the Button. Although I have some suited potential, the presence of Elon in the Big Blind also makes calling less attractive as it may lead to a three-way pot where my hand is significantly behind. Given the current stack sizes and my position, the most prudent move to preserve my stack is to fold.

T2h is a weak suited gapped connector. While button ranges are wide in late position, defending OOP from the BB with such a low hand risks difficult postflop spots where I'm out of position against Minni's frequent continuation betting observed in the history. Pot odds are decent (200 to call into 450, ~31% implied but low realization), but equity is marginal (~35-40% vs wide range) and playability poor with low cards easily dominated. Stack depth 38BB doesn't justify speculative call. Standard fold to conserve chips for better spots.

I am on the button with A5o. In a 3-handed game, this is a very strong starting hand. With Elon being short-stacked (37.5BB), I want to apply pressure. A standard raise to 3x the big blind is appropriate here to take control of the pot and potentially end the hand pre-flop, or set up a post-flop situation where I have the range advantage.

2c4c is a weak suited gapper with poor playability out of position. Facing an aggressive button raise from Flash (huge stack, history of frequent raises), and with Minni yet to act in BB (potential squeeze), the pot odds (250 to call into 450) aren't sufficient given the postflop OOP struggle and low equity vs likely calling range. Preserve stack for better spots; fold.

Flash (button) opened to 300. I’m in the big blind with 7♠9♥. Versus a button stealing range this is a marginal but defensible hand—too good to fold routinely but not strong enough to 3‑bet out of position. Pot odds: I must call 200 into a 450 pot (~30.8% equity needed), and 7‑9 offsuit has reasonable equity and postflop playability heads‑up. Plan: call to see the flop, play cautiously out of position—check/fold to heavy aggression when I completely miss, otherwise continue with pairs or good draws and look for cheap realizations or occasional turns to bluff. Total committed on this street will be 300 (including my posted BB).

I defended the big blind preflop against a button open with 7♠9♥ — marginal but reasonable to call heads‑up. The flop A♣ T♠ 3♠ misses my hand: I have no pair and only weak backdoor equity (a backdoor spade/straight route). Flash (preflop raiser, button) will c‑bet this board a high frequency, and I’m out of position with a dominated hand and only very small equity. I’ll check to control the pot and avoid building a big pot with air. Plan is to fold to any meaningful c‑bet (especially >= ~1/3–1/2 pot); I may consider calling very small probe bets to see a cheap turn, but otherwise look to realize equity cheaply or fold and wait for a better spot. For now: check.

I have flopped top pair with a weak kicker (Ace-Five). The board is Ace-Ten-Three, which is generally quite favorable for my pre-flop raising range. Minni checked to me, which is a standard play for the Big Blind on this texture. I should place a continuation bet here to gain value from weaker Aces (though I lose to many), flush draws, and gutshots like King-Jack or Queen-Jack. A half-pot bet of 350 is standard to protect my hand and define where I stand in the pot.

I defended the BB preflop with 7♠9♥ but the flop A♣ T♠ 3♠ misses my hand entirely. Flash bets 350 into a 1000 pot (~35% pot). Calling 350 would require roughly 350/(1000+350+350) ≈ 20.6% equity to break even. My equity here is essentially nil (only remote runner‑runner possibilities — ~4–5% at best). I’m out of position against a deep, aggressive opener who c‑bets a wide range, so calling is a losing long‑term play and a raise would be an overaggressive bluff with very little fold equity. Fold and conserve the 5800 stack for better spots.

64o is a very weak hand: low, offsuit, disconnected with poor playability and low equity against any reasonable range. Preflop on the button offers position, but facing a 100 to call into a 150 pot gives only about 2.5:1 implied odds initially (risk 100 to win 150), requiring roughly 40% equity which this hand doesn't have against limping/calling ranges from SB/BB. Opponents have deep stacks (Minni 57BB, Flash 303BB), but my 37BB stack doesn't justify speculative calls with trash. Folding preserves stack for better spots; no steal value here as pot is contested. Consistent with GTO folding low-percentage hands preflop.

7♠5♥ is a weak, marginal hand, but it’s very cheap to play heads‑up from the small blind. I only need to put another 50 to complete to 100 into a 150 pot, and in heads‑up I’ll have position post‑flop to realize equity or fold to aggression. Raising would bloat the pot with a poor hand and invite tougher decisions against Flash’s deep, often aggressive stack. I’ll complete to see a flop and re‑evaluate; fold to a large raise.

I am in the big blind with 8h7d. Elon has folded, and Minni has completed from the small blind. The pot is currently 200 and I have the option to check and see a free flop. Given that 87o is a decent speculative hand but not strong enough to merit a raise against a limper in a 3-handed game where stacks are relatively deep compared to the pot, checking is the most standard and solid play to realize equity without bloating the pot out of position.

Flop analysis: board 9♠ 3♦ 4♣, my hand 7♠5♥. I have a single gutshot to a 6 (4 clean outs → ~16.5% to make the straight by river). I also have three remaining 7s and three remaining 5s that would make a pair, so 10 total cards that improve me to at least a pair (~38% to improve by river), but many of those pair-outs will still be second‑best against likely BB holdings (9x, small-mid pairs, overcards that call).

Pot math and opponent: pot is 200, I have 5,700 and Flash has ~30,350. I'm out of position post‑flop. To profitably call a bet here I need substantial pot odds (a 100 chip bet requires ~25% equity; larger bets require even more). My clean equity to a winning hand is well below that vs a reasonable calling/raising range, so calling is generally a losing proposition. Flash is a deep, often aggressive player who can float and apply pressure; I don't want to bloat the pot OOP with a weak, one‑card draw.

Decision / plan: check. If Flash checks back I get a free turn to improve or take another look. If Flash bets a normal amount, I will fold—only consider calling an extremely tiny probe that gives correct pot odds, and re‑evaluate on the turn if I hit a 6 or pair. This preserves my stack and avoids difficult OOP decisions with marginal equity.

On a flop of 9s3d4c, I have 8-high with no direct draw. Minni checked as the small blind. While I could attempt to bluff and take down the small pot of 200, there is little reason to bloat this pot with absolute air when I have no equity to fall back on. I will check and take a free card to see if I can improve on the turn.

Board paired on the turn (9♠ 3♦ 4♣ 3♠). With 7♠5♥ I have only 10 clean river outs (three 7s, three 5s, four 6s) → 10/46 ≈ 21.7% raw chance to improve. Many of those outs will still be second‑best versus Flash’s likely calling/raising range. Pot is 200 and the min bet is 100, which would make the pot 300 and require ~33% equity to profitably call — I don’t have that. Only an unusually tiny probe (~50) would give close to correct pot odds, and Flash is a deep, often aggressive player who can apply pressure OOP. I won’t bloat the pot with a marginal one‑card draw out of position. Check and fold to any bet (re‑evaluate on the river if checked to).

I have 8-high on a board of 9s3d4c3s. Minni has checked both the flop and the turn. While I could attempt to bluff here to take down the small 200-chip pot, there is little incentive to risk chips with zero equity when I can simply take a free look at the river. If Minni has a weak pair or even a better high card, she might call a small bet, and I have no hand to fall back on. Checking back is the most prudent play to potentially realize some miraculous river equity or just see a cheap showdown.

I have nothing on the river (7♠5♥ on 9♠ 3♦ 4♣ 3♠ A♦) — no pair, no legitimate bluff-catcher. Flash checked flop and turn, which can be both a sign of weakness or a slow-play, but the Ace on the river gives him a lot of safe hands (Ax), pairs (9x, pocket pairs), and even a boat/set sweating out. The pot is tiny (200) relative to our stack (5,700) and Flash’s stack; any reasonable bet I make will be called by most of his value/one-pair range and I have very little fold equity. Betting to try to steal here (risking chips to win 200) is a poor EV play — a sizable bet is too risky and a tiny probe is likely to be called. Best line is to check and take the free showdown; if Flash fires a bet I will fold given my hand’s lack of showdown value.

The board is 9s 3d 4c 3s Ad and the pot is very small (200). Minni has checked all three streets after limping pre-flop. While my 8-high is unlikely to win at showdown, the pot is too small to justify a bluff, especially since the river Ace is a card that could easily have improved a weak limping range or been a card she's willing to call with. I'll check and take the loss if she has any pair or a better high card.

J♦5♥ is a marginal hand, but I'm on the button with position and good stack depth (57BB). The pot is only 150 with 50/100 blinds so raising to take the initiative and apply pressure to the blinds is standard. I’ll open to 3x the big blind (300 total) — large enough to generate fold equity against both the SB and the BB while still keeping the pot manageable if called. Plan: fold to a large 3-bet/shove, and if called use positional advantage to continuation-bet favorable flops and control the pot on dry or scary textures.

I am in the Small Blind with 73o, which is a very weak hand. Minni (the Button) has opened for a standard raise to 300. 73o is significantly below the threshold for a call or a 3-bet bluff from out of position, especially with a short-stacked opponent. Folding is the only sensible play here.

Preflop in the big blind facing a button raise to 300 from Minni after SB folds. Pot is 450, calling 200 more for odds of 2.25:1 (needing ~31% equity). Ad8c is a decent speculative hand with an ace blocker, decent high card strength, and good playability. Against a typical button opening range (wide, ~35-50% of hands), A8o has around 38-42% equity heads-up and performs well multiway but here it's heads-up. Postflop, I'm OOP but can check-raise strong hands or control pot with position advantage in realization. Folding is too tight given odds; 3-betting light risks variance OOP with 36bb stack. Minni has been aggressive but shown down value sometimes; call to realize equity and see flop.

On this flop of 4c7cJh, I have Ad8c for ace-high with a backdoor club flush draw and minimal straight potential. The board is somewhat drawy with two clubs and possible straight draws (56, T9, 85, etc.). As the OOP preflop caller in the BB vs Button aggressor, my range is perceived as capped somewhat but includes speculative hands like this. Minni's button opening range is wide (~40%+), and this flop misses much of it: no Jx for many, clubs hit suited connectors sometimes, but often air or weak pairs. Checking first is standard OOP with no strong hand or draw. I can check-call light if bet small (pot odds good, ~2:1 implied), check-fold to large bets, or check-raise if I improve or as semi-bluff later, but no bet now as it polarizes my range too much without equity realization. Folding impossible since no bet. Goal: control pot, realize thin equity vs wide range, avoid bloating pot OOP with marginal hand.

I have top pair (J♦) but a very weak 5-kicker on a two-club board (4♣7♣J♥). Elon called preflop and checked the flop, so his range includes missed broadways, medium pairs, slower pairs, and club draws. I want to both extract value from worse Jx and charge/price out club draws and turn equity. A firm c-bet of ~2/3 pot puts pressure on his draws and weaker holdings while keeping my options open. If he raises big I can fold (likely behind), if he just calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and often continue for value/protection on safe cards. Bet 400 into a 650 pot to achieve that plan.

Preflop call OOP with Ad8c was standard speculative play with good odds and playability against button wide range. Flop 4c7cJh gives ace-high with Ad blocker (blocks strong Ax) and backdoor club draw. Board favors Minni's wide button range (Jx pairs, weak clubs, straight draws like 56/T9/85) but misses many overcards, broadways, and Ax highs. Her 400 bet (~40-60% pot depending on dead SB money) is standard c-bet sizing into dryish but drawy board. Pot odds ~2.6:1 require ~27% equity; A-high has ~35-45% vs polarized c-bet range (value + bluffs). OOP call realizes equity thinly, controls pot size, allows turn fold to aggression on scary cards (club/straight completer). Raising semi-bluff risky OOP with marginal equity and no strong draw (only backdoor); folding too tight given price and blocker. Effective SPR low (~8 post-call), can reassess turn. Minni aggressive historically but check-folds sometimes; exploit by calling light.

On the turn, the board is 4c7cJh4h, pairing the fours. I hold Ad8c for ace-high with a weak club (no flush draw made or strong backdoor). Preflop call and flop check-call OOP were standard thin plays against button's wide range and good pot odds. The paired board improves sets/trips (44,J4,74 suited, etc.) but kills some straight draws like 5-6 (which now has paired board reducing equity). Minni's range (button raise/c-bet) includes value (top pair Jx, 77,44, clubs), bluffs (overcards, missed draws), and medium strength. Ace-high blocks strong Ax and has showdown value against some bluffs/weak pairs, but limited playability OOP. Pot 1450, my stack 3050 (SPR ~4:1 post-check), betting polarizes my range badly without strong equity/fold equity. Checking standard: realize equity by calling small valuebets or folding to aggression on scary board. Historical aggression from Minni suggests she may bet wide; exploit by stationing light OOP in low SPR spot. Avoid bloating pot OOP with marginal hand; prepare for river showdown if checked through.

I raised preflop and c-bet the flop (400 into ~650) and Elon called. Turn pairs the board (4h) which meaningfully reduces the value of my one-pair hand: my J5 has a very weak kicker and the paired 4 increases the chance someone has a set/full house or at least a made medium pair. Elon’s line (call preflop, call flop, check turn) looks like a range of medium pairs, worse Jx, slow-played sets, or missed/paired draws. Betting again here would build a pot where my kicker is likely dominated and could get me committed against better Jx or a set; a large bet also hands initiative to a short-stacked opponent who can shove. I want to control the pot size and see a river, allowing bluffs to unfold and avoiding tough fold/call/raise decisions now. Plan: check the turn, and on the river call reasonable-sized value/bluff bets from worse hands or fold to large shoves that represent a made boat/quads. If he checks back the river I can re-evaluate for a small value bet or take showdown.

River Ah pairs my Ad, giving me top pair (aces) with a jack kicker on a board of 4c7cJh4hAh. This significantly improves my hand from marginal ace-high bluff-catcher post-turn to strong value. Preflop and flop actions were thin calls OOP against Minni's wide button range (raises frequently, c-bets drawy boards). Turn 4h paired the board (good for sets/trips but kills some draws), and Minni's check behind suggests weakness: either capped medium strength (weak Jx, small pairs), missed draws (clubs like 56s/T9s/85s now bricked), or traps (unlikely given history). No flush completed (clubs missed), heart flush possible but requires specific two-heart hands in her range. Straights limited. My Ad blocks strong Ax value; 8c backdoor irrelevant now. Pot 1450, my stack 3050 (SPR ~2:1), betting extracts value from her worse Ax (A-high/KQ), weak pairs, and bluffs while protecting against unlikely rivers. Checking risks free showdown where she might have showdown value or check back weak; betting polarizes but thin value range includes weaker Ax/Jx. Size ~2/3 pot (975 rounds to 1000) balances value/bluff denial, low enough for calls from worse, high fold equity vs air. Minni's aggression history (bets rivers often) but turn check indicates caution; exploit by betting thin value OOP in low SPR spot to build stack.

Elon’s line (call preflop, call my 400 c‑bet on the flop, check the turn, then bet 1,000 on the river) is weighted toward value — Ax that just paired the river, Jx with a better kicker, or slow‑played sets — but it also contains missed club/heart draws that can bluff. My J♦5♥ is top pair with a very weak kicker and is behind most of his value combos, but the river bet of 1,000 into the ~1,450 pot (2,450 after his bet) gives me ~29% pot odds (1,000 to win ~3,450). Raising would over‑polarize my range and commit too much with a marginal kicker against a short stack who can shove or call with lots of hands that beat me; folding surrenders too much equity against bluffs. Following my turn plan — check the turn and call reasonable-sized river bets while folding to huge shoves — the +EV line here is to call and get to showdown rather than bluff‑raise or fold.

I am on the button with T6o. While it's a marginal hand, the blinds are short-stacked (Elon with 54.5 BB and Minni with 39 BB). However, both opponents have been playing quite aggressively and calling frequently from the blinds. Given T6o has poor post-flop playability and the risk of being 3-bet or called out of position is high with these stack sizes, I will opt for a simple fold and wait for a better spot to utilize my chip lead.

Preflop in SB vs BB after BTN fold. Unopened pot, ideal steal spot. Q9o is in SB stealing range (~50%+ hands), good equity vs calling range (~60% vs random), straight potential, Q blocker. Raise standard size 3xBB to 300 total for fold equity and initiative. Limp passive, fold too tight. Minni defends wide but pressure her stack.

Facing a 3x open from the SB to 300 while I’m in the big blind with 3♠2♦. This is one of the weakest hands possible — poor equity, no suit, and terrible playability out of position. Calling 200 into a 400 pot gives ~3:1 pot odds (≈25% required), but against a SB open and expected continuation betting my realized equity will be well below what’s needed and I’ll face difficult multi-street decisions. With 39 BB effective I can wait for much better spots (suited connectors, pairs, or broadways) rather than defend with garbage. Fold and preserve the stack.

Pocket deuces (22) on the button in a 3-handed game is a strong speculative hand with great implied odds for set mining, especially in position. The pot is 150 with 100 to call, offering decent odds already, but raising isolates, builds the pot, and takes advantage of position against the SB and BB. Opponents like Flash have been aggressive but have massive stacks, and Minni calls frequently based on history. Min-raise to 200 is too small; standard 3x BB raise to 300 total applies pressure and denies equity to worse hands. My stack is 56BB, sufficient for postflop play.

I’m in the small blind with Js6s facing a button open to 300 (pot 450) and the big blind (Flash) still to act. Js6s is a marginal, often-dominated hand with poor OOP playability — especially likely to become a multiway pot given Flash’s deep stack. Calling to see a flop OOP or making a light 3-bet here are both low EV lines; a min-raise risks a caller/4-bet and getting played off the hand, while calling hands like this multiway loses equity quickly. With ~38.5BB effective I’ll preserve my stack and fold to the open.

I am in the Big Blind with 8s7s, a strong suited connector. Elon, on the Button, has raised to 300. Given the stack depths (Elon has about 53 BBs and Minni has about 38 BBs), I have excellent implied odds to see a flop with a hand that plays very well post-flop. Calling 200 more into a pot that will be 750 (nearly 4:1 odds) is a standard and profitable play with this hand in this position.

I have bottom pair (sevens) on a board of King-high, Ten, and Seven. Elon raised from the button, so he could have a variety of hands, including Kings, Tens, or even straight draws like QJ or flush draws in hearts. My plan is to check and see how Elon reacts. Leading out into the pre-flop aggressor on this board doesn't provide much benefit with a marginal made hand. Checking allows me to control the pot size and potentially get to a cheap showdown or catch an improving card on the turn.

Pocket 22 is a weak underpair on a draw-heavy flop Kh-7h-Td, which connects strongly with Flash's BB defending range (Kx, Tx, suited connectors, flush/straight draws). Flash's check could be weakness, a trap, or inducing a c-bet. With position, checking back achieves pot control, realizes equity cheaply against potential bluffs/missed draws on later streets, and avoids bloating the pot with a marginal hand OOP on turn/river if I bet and get check-raised. My stack is 53BB, pot small at 650; no need to risk it pre-turn without improvement. History shows Flash aggressive but checking here suggests caution. Plan: check, reassess turn.

On the turn Queen of Clubs, the board is now very connected (K-Q-T-7). My bottom pair of sevens has very little relative value. Elon checked back the flop, which could indicate a marginal made hand or a total whiff. However, the Queen likely improves many of his Broadway cards. There is no reason to bet here as I won't get better hands to fold or worse hands to call. I will check and see if he checks back again, potentially taking me to a cheap showdown.

Pocket 22 on this board Kh-7h-Td-Qc is a marginal underpair with minimal showdown value against Flash's BB defending range, which includes Kx, Tx, Qx now, straight draws (JQ, 89), heart flush draws, and pairs like 88-99-JJ that flop decent equity. Flash checked flop and turn, which could represent weakness (missed draws, floats), a trap with strong hands, or inducing a bet from me as the preflop raiser. Betting risks bloating the pot OOP if facing resistance—no, IP actually (Button vs BB), but still, thin value at best vs his range, and vulnerable to check-raises from sets, two-pair, straights (KT, QJ hidden). Pot control is key: checking realizes equity cheaply on the river, pots ~650 with 53BB stack vs his 302BB monster stack, avoiding overcommitment without improvement. History shows Flash aggressive but passive here, suggesting possible weakness. Plan: check, evaluate river; fold to aggression or value bet if improves/brick checks through.

On the river, the board is Kh-7h-Td-Qc-8c, and I have made two pair (8s and 7s). Pre-flop, Elon raised from the button and I called. On both the flop and the turn, the action went check-check. Elon's passivity on a King-high flop and a Queen turn suggests he either has a marginal showdown value hand (like a Ten or a low pocket pair) or has missed the board entirely. While my two pair is relatively strong, the board is very connected, and any J9 would have made a straight. However, given Elon's checks, it's unlikely he's sitting on a monster. By checking the river, I allow Elon to bluff with his 'air' hands or check back his marginal hands, ensuring I get to a showdown without risk of being raised off the best hand if he happens to have something like J9 or a higher two pair.

On the river IP vs BB who has checked three streets on a coordinated board (Kh7hTdQc8c) with possible straights (e.g., J9 for 9-T-J-Q-K), higher pairs, two-pair combos, and completed heart/club draws in villain's OOP calling range. Pocket 22 is an underpair with some showdown value against air, missed draws (hearts now bricked somewhat), and weak Ax/high card hands that BB defends wide pre but passively checks through. Strong value hands (sets, straights, TP) likely bet at some point given history of Flash's aggression (e.g., Hand 2, 10, 46). Thin value bet risks check-raise from better and fold-out worse; villain's triple check suggests capping range low. Pot control optimal: check realizes equity cheaply with 53BB stack vs villain's 302BB monster, avoiding unnecessary risk in multiway potential future spots. Consistent with prior pot control checks on flop/turn. Plan: check to showdown, preserve stack for better spots over 150 hands.

I'm on the button in a 3-handed pot with T♥6♦ — a marginal hand but playable because I have position and the pot is small (150). Calling 100 is cheap, but open-raising to 300 (3x the BB) is the standard exploitative play here: it gives good fold equity against the blinds, gains the initiative, and keeps the pot manageable relative to my stack (3,850 ≈ 38.5BB). Flash (SB) is very deep and aggressive and Elon (BB) will defend, but a 300 sizing pressures their marginal defending ranges while allowing me to fold to a sizable 3-bet without a big loss. If called I'll use position to C-bet dry flops and fold when clearly out of line; if either blind folds I pick up the pot immediately. Raise to 300 maximizes my long-term EV in this spot versus just limping/calling with a weak offsuit ten.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Six offsuit. Minni opened for 300 from the Button. Q6o is a weak hand to defend out of position, and with my significant stack lead, there is no need to play marginal hands and potentially build a pot for the shorter stacks. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop in BB facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot 450, to call 200 more for great odds (~3.25:1). QTo is a playable hand with straight potential (JQKxx or 89Jxx), decent high card, and good playability heads-up against a wide button range. Minni raises frequently from history, often with speculative or marginal hands. Defending wide from BB vs BTN is standard GTO, and this has ~40%+ equity vs typical open range. Raising is possible as a squeeze since SB folded, but QTo is better as a call to realize equity on flops. Folding is too tight. Plan to call and play postflop cautiously OOP.

On this flop of 3h Jh 7d, holding Qh Tc, I have Queen-high with no pair, no strong draws (one heart for backdoor flush draw, distant backdoor straight potential with gutshot to Broadway needing runner-runner K-A). The board is somewhat coordinated with a heart flush draw possible and overcards likely in villain's wide Button opening range. As OOP first to act in a 650 pot vs an aggressive Minni who frequently raises preflop, check-fold is standard with this weak showdown value hand. Betting would be bluff-heavy and risky OOP against a capping range; checking realizes fold equity if Minni checks behind or allows controlled pot if she bets small (though I'll fold to most bets). Preserves stack for better spots given history of Minni's aggression postflop.

I opened preflop and have position; Elon (BB) called with a fairly wide range. The flop J♥ 3♥ 7♦ is fairly dry but two-toned. I hold T♥6♦ — no pair, only a very weak backdoor heart possibility, but I also block many heart/ten combos. Elon checked, so a continuation bet here will take down a lot of his missed broadways and small pairs. I want meaningful fold equity but don’t want to overcommit with air, so I’ll size up to ~60% pot to apply pressure (400 into 650). If he folds I collect; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn and only continue aggressively with improvement or clear weakness; I’ll fold to a strong check-raise given my current hand.

On this 3h Jh 7d flop, Qh Tc is Queen-high with minimal backdoor draws (one heart, distant gutshot Broadway potential runner-runner). No pair, no strong draw, poor showdown value. Facing Minni's 400 bet into ~650 pot (now 1050 pot), pot odds ~3.6:1 require ~22% equity, but vs her aggressive button c-bet range (wide: Ax suited, broadways, Jx, suited connectors, bluffs), I have ~25-35% equity tops, often less against polarized bets. OOP postflop after calling pre, folding to this bet is standard to deny her equity realization with better hands and avoid difficult turn spots where I'll face further aggression. Minni's history shows frequent postflop betting; check-fold preserves stack for premium spots in long 150-hand game. Raising as bluff too thin OOP vs aggro player who defends wide; calling commits too much without improving paths.

I am on the button with A9o, which is a very strong hand in a 3-handed game. My stack is significantly larger than both opponents, and they are playing relatively short-stacked (Elon ~50BB, Minni ~41BB). Raising is the standard play here to put pressure on the blinds and potentially win the pot immediately or see a flop with the best hand and position.

Preflop in small blind facing a button raise to 300 (250 to call into 450 pot). Holding 2c4d, a complete trash hand with no high card value, poor connectivity, offsuit, and terrible playability postflop especially OOP. Button ranges are wide but still crush this hand. BB has 41bb stack and can squeeze or call wide. No fold equity to a 3bet given stacks and positions. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots. Long-term EV maximization requires folding 80%+ of hands OOP preflop.

Flash (Button) opened to 300. I’m in the BB with K♥T♥ and 41bb effective. KTs is a standard defend vs a button open: it has good equity, suit/blockers and plays well postflop. Calling 200 (300 total committed) gives correct pot odds (200 into a 450 pot → ~31% required equity) and keeps the pot manageable — I avoid a thin 3-bet with a marginal but playable hand against a deep, capable opponent. Plan: see the flop and continue aggressively with hearts, top pair, or strong draws; fold or proceed cautiously on dry boards facing heavy aggression.

I defended preflop with K♥T♥, but the flop A♣ 9♦ 5♦ misses me — no pair and only a remote backdoor heart flush (requires both turn + river). Button’s opening range contains lots of A-x and hands that dominate me, and I’m out of position. With ~39bb effective and a 650 pot, checking controls the pot, keeps my exploitability low, and lets me evaluate Flash’s intentions. Plan: check and fold to heavy aggression, call small c-bets occasionally to see a turn only when the sizing and line make sense (or if I pick up equity).

I have top pair with a decent kicker (A9) on a board of A-9-5. This is an extremely strong hand, especially in a 3-handed game. My opponent (Minni) checked to me, which is standard. I should bet for value and to protect against potential diamond draws. Given the pot is 650, a bet of around half the pot is appropriate to extract value from weaker Aces or draws while maintaining control of the hand size.

I’m OOP in the BB with K♥T♥ on A♣9♦5♦. Flash’s bet 350 into a 1000 pot requires ~26% equity to justify a call (350/(1000+350) ≈ 25.9%). I have ~6 clean outs to pair K or T by the river (~24% to pair by river) and I can also realize additional fold equity by floating against a small c‑bet — his sizing is small and consistent with a wide BTN c‑bet range. Raising as a bluff is unattractive: I have no pair, limited clean equity and I’m out of position. Calling 350 (650 total committed) is a low-risk way to see a turn and either realize equity, float for a later stab if he slows, or fold to clear strength. I will fold to heavy aggression on the turn, but if I pair a K/T or villain checks I can proceed more aggressively.

Check. I have K♥T♥ on A♣ 9♦ 5♦ 5♥ — no pair and no real redraw (a flush is impossible with one card to come). Flash opened BTN and c‑bet small on the flop, then checked the turn, which is consistent with a wide/marginal checking range (missed c‑bets, some Ax slowplays, or pot‑control hands). I have only ~6 clean outs to pair (≈24% to pair by river) and no fold‑equity from a turn bet worth the risk — a bluff here would frequently be called by Kx, Qx, and many Ace and diamond combos, and I’d be committing more stack with poor equity. Checking keeps the pot manageable, preserves stack flexibility, and lets me realize my showdown equity or look for a better river spot to bluff (or value‑bet if I pair). Plan: check to river, fold to large river aggression unless I pair, and consider a targeted bluff/value bet on a river that changes the board texture in my favor.

I currently hold two pair, Aces and Nines (Ad Ac 9s 9d), with a 5h from the board. Although the 5 paired on the turn, my two pair is still very strong in a 3-handed game. My opponent (Minni) checked twice, suggesting she might have a weaker Ace, a Nine, or a diamond draw. I will bet 750 into the 1350 pot to extract value from those hands while protecting against a potential diamond draw. This bet also sets up a potential all-in on the river given her remaining stack.

Fold. I’m out of position with K♥T♥ on A♣ 9♦ 5♦ 5♥ facing a 750 turn bet into a 2100 pot. To justify a call I need roughly 26% equity (750 / (2100+750) ≈ 26.3%). On the turn I only have ~6 clean outs (3 Ks + 3 Ts) — about 6/46 ≈ 13% to pair on the river — well below the required equity. I cannot make a flush or a reliable straight by the river, and any K/T pair will frequently still lose to Ax or a 5x that fit the BTN’s line (open, small flop c-bet, turn barrel). Raising as a bluff (min raise to 1500) is too thin: it commits a large portion of my 3550 stack with poor blockers and limited fold equity. Given the math and the opponent’s line, folding preserves stack and waits for a better spot.

J2 offsuit is a very weak hand with poor playability, low equity against calling ranges, and minimal straight or flush potential. Even on the button facing a small to-call into a modest pot, the postflop OOP disadvantage against blinds outweighs the odds. Opponents like Flash are deep and aggressive, Minni capable; better to fold trash and wait for playable spots to maximize stack long-term.

I have JsQs in the small blind with only the big blind left to act (Elon folded). This is a strong, suited Broadway hand that plays well both for value and postflop maneuvering. I don’t want to just complete (call 50) and play OOP with initiative surrendered, nor do I want to min-raise to 200 which is too small and invites loose calls/3-bets. I’ll raise to 300 total (≈3x the BB) — a standard, table-consistent sizing that isolates the BB, gives fold equity, denies good odds to speculative hands, and keeps the pot manageable with my ~35BB stack. Postflop I’ll c-bet good textures, control the pot on unfavorable boards, and re-evaluate versus aggression (call or fold vs large 3-bets depending on range/read).

I am in the Big Blind with T5o. Minni has raised to 300 from the Small Blind. Given the current pot odds, I need to call 200 more into a pot of 400. However, T5o is a very weak hand even against a wide SB opening range. Both opponents are extremely short-stacked (less than 50 BBs), which means implied odds are low and I'll likely face high-pressure decisions post-flop. I've been playing conservatively and protecting my stack, so folding here is the most disciplined move to avoid unnecessary variance with a bottom-tier hand.

I have Qd3s on the button in a 3-handed pot (pot 150, to call 100). Q3o is a weak holding, but being on the button I can open a very wide range to use my position and fold equity. I’ll raise to 300 total (roughly a 3x BB open) — larger than the min-raise to discourage callers and give me better chance to take the pot preflop or play heads-up in position. If I face a meaningful 3‑bet I will fold this hand; if called I’ll c-bet favorable flops and proceed cautiously when I miss.

I am in the Small Blind with Qs 9h. Minni, who is on the button, has raised to 300. Minni is extremely short-stacked with only 3350 chips total. Q9o is a decent hand in a 3-handed game, but out of position against a short-stack who is likely looking to commit. However, given the pot odds (calling 250 more to win a total pot of 450+300+100=850) and the wide range button players typically raise with, a call is reasonable. Raising might force a shove from Minni that I don't necessarily want to call, so I will call and see a flop.

In the big blind facing a button raise to 300 from Minni and a small blind call from Flash (who defends wide from SB), with pot 700 and needing to call 200 more for 3.5:1 odds. Kh6h is suited with king high, offering playability for flush draws, straight potential, and domination potential. Multiway OOP but odds justify calling a small amount (4% of stack). Folding loses dead money already invested; raising too thin against deep Flash. Plan to play postflop cautiously, looking for improvement on flush/straight draws or top pair.

I have middle pair (Nines) on an Ace-high board in a three-way pot. While my hand has some value, the presence of the Ace and being out of position against both opponents makes checking the most prudent action. This allows me to see how the pre-flop aggressor (Minni) and the Big Blind (Elon) respond to the board. If Minni bets, I will have to decide based on her sizing, but for now, checking to control the pot size with a medium-strength hand is the standard play.

Faced with an ace-high flop (7d9cAs) multiway OOP after calling preflop with Kh6h suited. Hand is king-high with no pair, no flush draw active (no hearts on board), and minimal straight potential (no strong draw). Flash (SB) checked, preserving my option for a free turn card. Betting into two opponents with air is -EV; no value and poor bluffing spot due to ace blocking strong hands but king not dominating enough, and deep stacks behind. Minni (button raiser) likely to bet frequently here. Check to realize equity, control pot, and reassess turn. Consistent with preflop plan to play cautiously postflop.

Flop is A♠ 9♣ 7♦ and I hold Q♦3♠ — I missed completely. I was the preflop raiser in a 3‑way pot, both opponents checked to me, so I can leverage my position and fold equity to try to take this pot down. Against two callers I should size cautiously to avoid bloating the pot with a bluff, so a small continuation bet (~1/3 pot) is appropriate: it represents an Ace/strong hand, pressures weak overcards and low pairs, and keeps my options open. If I get called I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn and pot‑control; if I face a substantial raise I will fold to preserve my stack. Betting 300 keeps the pot manageable while maximizing chances to win it now.

On this Ace-high board, I have middle pair (Nines). Minni, the pre-flop aggressor, has made a small bet of 300 into a 900 pot (1/3 pot sizing). Given the pot odds (300 to call for a total pot of 1500), I am getting 4:1 on a call. Minni is short-stacked and frequently aggressive pre-flop, so her continuation bet doesn't necessarily mean she has the Ace. I have enough value to call one street here to see if the turn improves my hand or if the action slows down, especially since Elon is still to act behind me. If I call and Elon folds, I'll be heads-up against Minni's likely wide range.

Facing a bet of 300 into 1500 pot (5:1 odds) multiway on an ace-high flop (7d9cAs) with Kh6h suited. Hand has zero equity: no pair, no flush draw (board rainbow, no hearts), weak backdoor straight/flush potential. Initial check was correct to control pot OOP with air. Flash's call from SB OOP suggests strength (aces, 87s+, sets, combo draws like JTs, Axs). Minni's button bet is wide but value-heavy often. Committing 300 more commits me to larger future bets with trash; equity <15% vs calling ranges. Proper fold to preserve stack for better spots. Deep effective stacks (min 3050 mine) don't change fold equity denial here.

On the turn, the board pairs with a Seven. I still hold middle pair (Nines) on an Ace-high board. Since I am out of position against the pre-flop aggressor, Minni, who is also short-stacked, checking is the most appropriate action. This allows me to see if she continues her aggression or checks back. If she checks, I likely have the best hand; if she bets, I will need to evaluate the sizing and my pot odds, keeping in mind she only has about two pot-sized bets remaining in her stack.

Turn pairs the board (7♦ 9♣ A♠ 7♠). I hold Q♦3♠ — I missed completely. I was the preflop raiser and made a small flop c-bet that was called; Flash’s calling range from the SB includes lots of A-x, 9-x, 7-x and pocket pairs, many of which either beat me now or will simply call a second barrel. The turn 7 dramatically reduces my fold equity, so firing again would be a very thin bluff and would risk a big chunk of my ~30.5bb stack for little expected gain. I’ll control the pot and check to the river, preserving my stack and giving myself the opportunity to call a small river bluff or fold to a meaningful bet. Re-evaluate on the river (call small, fold large; only bluff-catch if I pair up).

On the river, the board is 7d-9c-As-7s-Qh. My hand is Qs-9h, giving me two pair: Queens and Nines (Q-Q-9-9-A). I must be careful because any Ace now has Aces and Sevens (A-A-7-7-Q), which outranks my two pair. Minni checked the turn, which could indicate she doesn't have an Ace, or she's pot-controlling. However, the river Queen is a great card for me as it improves my middle pair to the best possible two pair involving my hole cards, beating any other single Queen (like KQ or QJ) and all pocket pairs below Aces. Given Minni's short stack and the fact that she checked the turn, I will check the river to induce a bluff from her missed draws or to see a cheap showdown, as betting might only get called by hands that beat me (like an Ace or a Seven).

I raised preflop and c-bet the flop; both checked the turn and Flash checked the river. The Q on the river pairs my Q♢3♠ giving me top pair with a very weak kicker. Flash’s line (call flop, then passive on turn/river) looks like a wide, medium‑strength calling range (lots of A‑x, 9‑x, single pairs and some pocket pairs) — many of those hands are behind my pair. Checking here surrenders value; a large bet would fold out the worse hands I want to extract from and a tiny bet keeps the pot controllable if I run into strength. I’ll make a small value bet of ~1/3 pot (500) to get called by Ax and other single‑pair hands while minimizing risk. If Flash check‑raises big I’ll assume a polarized, stronger range (two‑pair/trips or a bluff I can’t comfortably call) and fold; if he calls I go to showdown.

On the river board of 7d-9c-As-7s-Qh, I have improved to two pair, Queens and Nines (Qs-Qh-9h-9c-As). Minni bet the flop, checked the turn, and is now betting 500 into a 1500 pot on the river. Her turn check suggests she might not have a strong Ace or a Seven, as those hands would often continue betting for value or protection on a drawing board. While any Ace (Aces and Sevens) or any Seven (Trip Sevens) still beats me, my improvement to Queens and Nines beats her potential Nines, lower pocket pairs, or missed straight draws. Given the pot odds of 4:1 (calling 500 to win a total pot of 2000) and her turn check, a call is the most mathematically sound decision to see if my two pair is good.

I am on the button with Q6o. In a three-handed game, this hand is marginal. Both opponents are significantly shorter than me (Elon with 4,600 and Minni with 2,450). Given Minni's short stack, she is in a position where she may frequently shove over a button raise. Q6o does not perform well against a shoving range. Since I have a massive stack lead, there is no need to play weak hands out of position or risk chips unnecessarily against short-stack desperation moves. I will fold and wait for a stronger opening hand.

Preflop, folded to me in the SB with K5o offsuit. This is a marginal hand at best. While it has some high card value and blocker, the poor kicker and offsuit nature make it unsuitable for raising OOP against the BB. Limping would put me in a tough spot postflop out of position heads-up, facing a wide BB defending range. Standard play is to raise or fold from the SB in this spot, and K5o doesn't warrant a raise. Pot odds are favorable for calling (50 to 150, ~25% equity needed), but postflop playability OOP is poor, and Minni's history shows she calls stations but also bets weak hands. To maximize EV long-term over 150 hands, folding weak hands preflop preserves stack for better spots. Minni's stack is 2450 (effective), mine 4600. Folding is disciplined.

3-handed play on the button facing only the blinds (SB completed or limped effectively, pot 150). Qs6d is a playable hand from this position: queen-high blocker, potential for top pair or draws postflop, and excellent position to realize equity. Standard play is to raise a wide range to steal blinds or build pot in position. Minni has a shorter stack (2550), Flash deep but aggressive. Raising to 3x BB (300) applies pressure and takes initiative. Folding too tight; calling limping range too loose preflop.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the small blind with 8h6h and ~25.5BB. 8‑6 suited is a speculative but playable hand for one street — good for flush/connector equity — and the price to see a flop is reasonable (I need to add 250 more). A min 3‑bet to 500 is too thin here: it bloats the pot out of position and invites a squeeze from the very deep BB (Flash). Shoving is unnecessary with this hand/stack. I’ll call to control the pot, realize equity, and evaluate on the flop; fold to heavy action on dry/unfavorable boards and look to pot‑build only if I hit strong draws or pairs.

I am in the Big Blind with a small pocket pair [22]. The Button (Elon) has raised to 300 and the Small Blind (Minni) has called. I only need to put in 200 more to call into a pot of 700, giving me 4.5:1 odds. Given the short stacks of my opponents (43 BB and 23 BB), my implied odds for set-mining are lower than usual, but the immediate pot odds make this a standard call to see a flop. Raising would likely commit me against their shoving ranges, so calling is the most prudent play.

I check to control the pot. With 8♥6♥ on 7♥7♠3♣ in a three-way pot I have no pair and only a very weak backdoor heart draw (need both turn and river hearts). I’m out of position to the preflop raiser (Elon) and a very deep BB (Flash), so a c-bet here will often get called and put me in tough spots vs stronger ranges. Pot is already ~900 and my stack is ~2,300 (~23BB) — not worth bloating the pot with air. Plan: check and fold to any sizable bet (especially from Elon); consider calling a very small probe to see one more card, and only try a bluff/stab if the turn brings a heart or a very favorable card and action looks weak.

I have Deuces (22) on a 7h-7s-3c flop. This is a very dry board. I checked to the pre-flop aggressor (Elon) as per standard procedure. Minni has also checked. My hand is currently third pair/underpair. Given the board texture, it's unlikely anyone has a 7, although Elon or Minni could easily have a 7 in their ranges (78s, A7s). If it checks through, I'm happy to see a free turn. If Elon bets, I'll have to evaluate his sizing and the likelihood of him continuation betting with overcards. For now, since it is my turn and Minni checked, checking is the standard play here.

Preflop raiser on a dry paired flop (77-3 rainbow) that hits few calling ranges but favors my button raising range (blocks QQ, AKQ, etc.). Both opponents checked, indicating weakness or traps, but their histories show frequent check-folds (e.g., Hand 2, 28, 32). Qs6d is a bluff candidate with queen-high blocker, backdoor spade draw (Qs + 7s), and excellent position to realize equity if called. C-bet 40% pot applies fold equity, denies equity to overcards/draws, and pot controls. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or letting them realize equity cheaply. Minni's shorter stack (2300) adds fold pressure.

Flop: 7h 7s 3c; I have 8h6h — no pair, only backdoor hearts (need both turn+river) and two-card pair outs (three 8s + three 6s). Pot is 1300 and Elon fires a small bet of 400. Calling 400 into a 1300 pot gives me ~23.5% required equity. My roughly 24% chance to hit a pair by the river (plus a tiny chance of runner-runner hearts) lines up with the pot odds, so a call to see the turn is justified. Elon opened from the button and often has a wide c-bet range here; this size looks like a probe rather than a polar value shove. I will call to realize equity and keep the pot controllable; I will fold to significant turn aggression unless I improve (pair an 8/6 or get a favorable heart turn that creates a meaningful draw), and I won’t bluster-raise out of position with air given the very deep BB and a calling button.

On this paired board of 7h-7s-3c, I have the lowest possible underpair (22). Elon's continuation bet into two players is standard, but Minni's call is a significant red flag. Her call suggests she has a piece of the board, a higher underpair (like 44-66), or a 7. Since I am third to act and facing both a bet and a call, my hand has virtually no showdown value and very little equity to improve. Calling 400 into a 1700 pot might seem attractive on price, but I would be out of position and likely forced to fold to any further aggression on the turn. Folding is the most disciplined play.

Turn decision: I hold 8♥6♥ on 7♥ 7♠ 3♣ J♦. I have no pair and only 6 clean outs (three 8s, three 6s) to improve on the river — ~6/46 ≈ 13% equity. Pot is 1,700 and my stack ≈1,900 (~19BB). Betting out of position on the turn with such weak equity is a marginal bluff: I have poor fold equity versus a button who opened and c-bet the flop, and a raise would put me in a very uncomfortable, likely losing spot. Optimal, lower-variance line is pot control: check and fold to meaningful aggression. If Elon checks back I’ll reevaluate the river; if he fires a small bet I’ll consider a call only if the price is attractive relative to my ~13% chance to pair. For now I check to keep the pot manageable and avoid committing chips with air.

Turn card Jd is a relatively blank card on this paired 7s board (77-3-J rainbow), not completing strong draws and potentially improving some weaker hands like Jx or gutshots minimally. Minni checks again after calling flop c-bet, indicating capped range: likely overcards (KQo+, Ax), weak 7s (like 76, 75), or floats/small pairs below 7s. My preflop button raise + flop cbet reps strong hands (sets, 77, overpairs), and Qs6d provides a queen-high blocker (blocks QJ, Q7) and backdoor spade potential. With 3900 stack and position vs Minni's 1900 effective stack, betting ~half-pot (900) applies max fold equity, denies equity to her calling range, and builds pot if she proceeds weakly. Checking risks giving free river cards to overcards/draws or multiway realization. Her history shows check-call tendencies but folds to continued aggression (e.g., Hands 28, 42, 51). High EV bluff spot to realize fold equity now.

Fold. Elon’s turn bet is 900 into a 2600 pot; calling 900 to win 3500 requires ~25.7% equity. I hold 8♥6♥ on 7♥7♠3♣J♦ with no pair and only six clean outs (three 8s + three 6s) ≈ 13% to hit by the river (runner‑runner hearts are negligible). Preflop opener → small c‑bet on the flop → sizable turn bet is a line consistent with value (Jx, a set, or a pair/overpair), not a pure probe I can profitably chase. Calling would also leave me short‑stacked (~1,000) and very vulnerable to a shove. Given the math, my earlier plan to check and fold to meaningful turn aggression, and the lack of fold equity OOP, the +EV play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

I’m on the button with J♠8♣ (J8o), stack 1,900 (~19 BB). Pot is 150 and it’s 100 to call. J8o is a marginal but playable button hand — in position it realizes equity and is a good candidate to open-steal. I want initiative and to fold out the blinds often, so I’ll raise to 300 (3x the big blind). This sizing is standard at the table, gives solid fold equity, keeps the pot manageable with my stack depth, and lets me play postflop as the aggressor. If I face a large 3-bet I’ll usually fold; if I get single-called I’ll c-bet favorable flops and leverage position.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Six offsuit. Minni, on the button, has raised to 3x the big blind. With her stack being very short (only 1,600 chips total), she is likely playing a wide range of hands to try and double up. A6o is strong enough to defend the blind against a button open, but not necessarily strong enough to 3-bet for value given she might shove over the top. I will call the additional 250 chips to see a flop and evaluate based on how it hits my hand and her remaining stack.

2c3s is one of the weakest hands possible, offsuit, disconnected, no high card value. Facing a button raise from Minni (who often raises wide) and a call from Flash in the small blind, the pot is multiway with strong ranges ahead. Pot odds are about 3.5:1 (200 to call into 700), but my equity is minimal (<20% multiway), poor playability postflop, and risking 3.6% of my 5500 stack for little gain. Standard fold from BB to preserve stack for better spots in this long 150-hand game.

I have the Ace of Spades on a King-high board with two spades. This gives me the nut flush draw back-door potential and one overcard. Since I am out of position against the pre-flop raiser, checking to the aggressor is the standard and correct play. I will see how Minni acts with her short stack.

I opened from the button and was single‑called; the flop 4♠-8♠-K♥ gives me middle pair (pair of 8s) and a spade blocker. Flash checked, so his calling range from the small blind is wide and contains many worse hands (smaller pairs, 4x, A/Q/J highs) and some draws. This is a good spot to continuance-bet for value and protection while keeping the pot manageable given my 1,600 stack (~16 BB). I’ll make a half‑pot c‑bet (~350) to extract from worse hands and deny free cards to backdoor draws. If Flash check‑raises big or jams I’ll seriously consider folding and re‑evaluating; if he just calls I can use position to control the turn.

I have Ace-high with the nut flush blocker (Ace of Spades) on a King-high board. Minni has a very short stack remaining (1,250 chips) relative to the pot (1,050 total after her bet). If I call the 350, she will have 900 left and will likely shove on the turn. My current hand equity is low against a range that includes any King, Eight, or pocket pair. While I have a backdoor nut flush draw and one overcard, I am out of position against an opponent who is essentially pot-committed. Given my large chip lead in the game, I prefer to fold and avoid a marginal situation against a short-stacked, aggressive player.

I am on the Button with a very weak hand (Q3o). Both the Small Blind (Elon) and Big Blind (Minni) are playing relatively short stacks compared to the starting stack and my current lead. Q3o is not a hand that plays well post-flop, and opening into short stacks who are likely to defend aggressively or shove is not profitable here. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop in SB vs BB unopened pot (Button folded). Hand Jc6s is offsuit, gapped, with poor postflop playability out of position. While SB ranges are wide, J6o is below standard completing range due to dominated potential, low equity realization OOP, and reverse implied odds. Minni's stack is 22BB, but folding preserves stack for better spots in long 150-hand game. Pot odds (50 to call into 150) require ~25% equity, but hand plays poorly postflop against BB defense range shown in history (frequent calls but also folds to aggression). Raising not justified without suited/broadway strength.

Preflop, unraised pot with SB likely folded (pot exactly 150 from blinds), BB checked, heads-up on the button vs deep-stacked Flash. Excellent stealing spot with position postflop. 2h8d is weak but has fold equity against a checked BB range, which is often weak. Raising steals the 150 pot frequently, and if called, we have position to realize equity or bluff further. Standard button steal sizing around 3x BB.

I’m in the small blind with A♣3♥ and a short stack (2,300 ≈ 23bb) facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and it’s 250 to call. With this stack depth I want to maximize immediate fold equity and avoid difficult out-of-position postflop decisions. A3o is a reasonable shove hand — it blocks many strong Aces and has decent heads-up equity versus a wide open-raising range. A tiny reraise (to 500) doesn’t isolate and calling keeps me OOP against two players. Shoving either wins the pot preflop or gets me to a simple all-in showdown with reasonable equity. Commit entire stack (2,300 total).

Minni has committed almost her entire stack with a large 3-bet from the small blind. Qs 7s is a speculative hand that does not perform well against a 3-betting range, which typically consists of high pairs and strong Ax hands. Additionally, Elon is still in the hand and could potentially shove his remaining chips. Calling 2,200 chips here with Q7s is a negative expected value play. Folding is the most disciplined and correct decision.

Preflop, after my button steal raise to 300 with 2h8d, Minni 3-bets massive to 2300 from SB. Her sizing is enormous (~8x my raise), screaming strength with premiums like QQ+, AK. My 28o is trash, ~25% equity vs that range at best. Pot 2700, call 2000 leaves 2850 behind (~1:1 SPR), unplayable with weak hand even in position. No fold equity 4-bet vs her, stacks not suited for bluff. Instant fold to avoid spew.

I’m on the button with J♦Q♦ — a strong, playable suited broadway with good blockers and great positional advantage. Pot is 150 and it’s only 100 to call, but the table standard open size has been about 300 and that sizing works well here: it pressures the blinds, builds a pot when I have position, and keeps post‑flop flexibility with my ~27.5bb stack. Flash (SB) is very deep/aggressive and Elon (BB) has ~50bb and will 3‑bet occasionally, so I’ll use a standard 3x open to 300. If I get called I plan to c‑bet favorable boards (especially diamond/connected/broadway flops); if I face a sizable 3‑bet I’ll re‑evaluate (call small 3‑bets to see a flop, fold to large 4‑bet shoves given my stack).

I am in the Small Blind with 8h6h, a decent suited connector. Minni, who has a very short stack of 2450, raised to 300 from the Button. Given my large stack of over 32,000 and the pot odds (calling 200 into a pot of 450), this is a standard call to see a flop. I have the bankroll to absorb the loss if I miss, and 86s plays well post-flop, especially against a short stack where I can put maximum pressure if I hit a piece of the board.

Qh6s is a weak offsuit hand with poor post-flop playability and low equity multiway. Facing a button raise from Minni and a call from Flash in SB, entering a 3-way pot OOP requires strong cards. Pot odds are decent (200 to win 700, ~28% needed), but the hand performs poorly against typical calling/raising ranges. Standard play is to fold to preserve stack for better spots. Effective SPR low with stacks, but no value in calling.

The flop comes Ad-Ah-2c, which is a very dry, paired board that significantly favors the pre-flop raiser's range (Minni). I have 8h6h, which has missed completely, though it does have very thin backdoor flush possibilities. Given Minni's short stack and the board texture, I will check and likely fold to any significant bet, as she is likely to continuation bet this board with many of her holdings, including those that already beat me.

Flop is 2♣ A♦ A♥ and I have Q♦J♦ — I have no pair and only a very thin backdoor diamond possibility (needs both turn and river). Flash (SB) called preflop and checked the flop; his calling range will include lots of A‑x, pocket pairs and other hands that either have a lot of equity here or can comfortably continue vs a c‑bet. This is a textbook pot‑control spot: a continuation bet would be thin and is likely to be called or raised by hands that dominate me. I’ll check to keep the pot small, avoid getting exploited by a very deep/aggressive opponent, and re‑evaluate on the turn. If Flash leads out with a small probe I may call to see the turn; I’ll fold to large aggression. If he checks back I can pick my spots on the turn (diamond/queen/jack or a blank to try a delayed bluff).

The flop went check-check on a very dry board (Ad Ah 2c). This suggests that Minni, the pre-flop raiser, likely does not have an Ace or a strong pocket pair, as those hands would typically continuation bet to build the pot or protect against draws. The turn 4d doesn't change much. Since I have 8-high and no real equity, my only way to win is to bluff. A half-pot bet of 350 should be enough to test her strength and potentially force a fold from her air or weak middle-range hands that currently beat me.

Board: 2♣ A♦ A♥ 4♦. I hold J♦ Q♦ — no pair but a four‑card diamond draw (9 clean diamond outs) plus backdoor pair outs (three Qs and three Js, though many of those are not clean vs an Ace-heavy range). Flash checked the flop and now makes a small turn bet (350 into ~1050), which looks like a probe or a thin value bet. Calling costs 350 for a pot of 1400, so I’m getting ~25% break‑even odds; pure flush equity (9/46 ≈ 19.6%) is a bit below that, but adding the Q/J outs and the fact I’m in position pushes my realistic equity closer to or above the price. Raising here would be a large, thin semi‑bluff against a very deep, aggressive player and would commit too many chips with marginal equity. Folding surrenders reasonable equity for a small price. I’ll call to realize my draw in position, keep the pot controlled, and re‑evaluate on the river (look to value or shove a diamond; fold to heavy action on a blank).

The board has double-paired (Aces and Deuces) with a 4. I hold 8-high, so my best hand is two pair, Aces and Deuces, with an 8 kicker (AA228). Minni's range for raising from the button and calling a turn bet likely includes many hands with kickers higher than an 8 (K, Q, J, T, 9) or pocket pairs that would form a better two pair (like AA554). Since the board is so dry and she called the turn, it's unlikely she'll fold a better hand to a river bet. Checking allows me to reach showdown cheaply or fold if she bets a significant amount, as I am likely behind her range.

Final board A♦ A♥ 2♣ 4♦ 2♥. My J♦Q♦ gives me A,A,2,2 with a Q kicker — two pair. Flash called preflop, checked the flop and made a very small turn stab (350) that I called; his river check is ambiguous but makes strong full-house/quads less likely (he would often bet for value). A river bet from me would be thin — there are few worse hands that will call and many better hands that will raise or already have me crushed. With the pot at 1,400 and my effective stack 2,100, I don’t want to inflate the pot with a marginal/value-thin line against a deep, aggressive opponent. I’ll check to reach showdown and realize my two‑pair equity rather than risk a costly confrontation.

I am on the button with Ten-Eight suited, a solid speculative hand. Both opponents are significantly shorter-stacked than I am. A standard 3x open raise puts pressure on them while allowing me to realize my positional advantage if they call.

T8o is a marginal offsuit hand with no high card strength or playability out of position. Facing a button raise from Flash, who has been aggressive in prior hands (frequent raises and c-bets), this is an easy fold preflop. Pot odds are ~1.8:1 (250 to call into 450 pot), but equity against a typical raising range is too low, especially OOP with multiway potential if BB calls. Preserve stack (50bb) for better spots to maximize long-term EV.

I'm in the big blind with Qc6d facing a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and I need to add 200 more (I'll have 300 total committed). Q6o is marginal but has reasonable equity against a wide BTN opening range and the pot odds (~31% required) make a defend profitable often. I don't want to commit more with a weak, OOP hand by 3-betting; folding is too tight given the odds. I'll call to see a flop and proceed cautiously — fold to heavy aggression on dry boards or continue selectively on favorable textures.

Check. I have top pair (Q) with a very weak 6-kicker on Q♠ 2♦ A♣ — a hand that is often behind any Ax and vulnerable to strong Qx/Ax combos in the button’s preflop range. The board is fairly dry (no real straights, only a single spade), so leading here has poor fold equity and just invites large raises from hands that already beat me. Checking controls the pot (pot is 650, my stack 3200), lets worse hands bluff or c-bet small, and keeps me from bloating the pot OOP with a marginal holding. Plan: call small-to-medium continuation bets (≈<=1/2 pot) to extract value and fold to large, polarizing raises; if Flash checks back I’ll reassess the turn and look to get value on safe turn cards or pot-control on scary ones.
