On this Ace-high board, I have a gutshot straight draw (needing a Jack) and a backdoor diamond draw with my Ten-Eight of diamonds. As the pre-flop aggressor, a continuation bet is standard here to represent the Ace or Queen. Minni checked, and since she has a relatively short stack (3200), a small bet of around 250-300 can often take the pot immediately or set up a profitable turn if a diamond or a Jack rolls off. If she calls, I still have equity to improve.

I have Qc6d on Qs 2d Ac — top pair but a very weak kicker and no realistic backdoor (with only one club on board and one in hand I cannot make a 5‑card club flush by the river). Flash opened wide from the button preflop and his small flop stab of 250 into a 900 pot is a low‑frequency, high‑range c‑bet that will include lots of Ax, some Qx, and plenty of bluffs. Calling 250 to try to realize equity is cheap (≈250 to win ~1400, ≈18% required equity) and keeps the pot under control OOP. Raising here would polarize my range, commit chips with a bad kicker, and create difficult decisions against a very deep, aggressive opponent — folding is too tight given the price. Plan: call the small bet to evaluate the turn; fold to heavy turn aggression that represents an Ace/strong Q, but continue versus small turn bets or check‑backs where I can extract value on safe cards or pot‑control.

I have top pair (Q) with a very weak 6 kicker on Q♠ 2♦ A♣ 4♦. Villain opened wide from the button and fired a small flop c‑bet that I called; his check on the turn is consistent with pot‑control with Ax/Qx or a missed c‑bet/bluff. Betting OOP with this marginal kicker has poor fold equity versus value hands (Ax, better Qx) and can put me in a tough spot if he raises. Pot control is the priority — pot is 1,150 and my stack is 2,950 — so I’ll check and force him to show aggression. Plan: check and call a small/medium river bet as a bluff‑catcher, but fold to large, polarized river aggression that represents an Ace or a stronger Q; if he checks back I’ll re‑evaluate the river and consider a small value bet on a safe card.

On the turn, the Four of Diamonds gives me a flush draw to go with my air. Minni checked again after calling my flop continuation bet. Since I was the pre-flop aggressor and continued on the flop, betting here maintains the narrative that I have a strong hand (like an Ace). With a flush draw, I have 'semi-bluff' equity—if she calls, I still have about a 20% chance to hit a winning flush on the river. A bet of around half the pot (600) puts pressure on her marginal holdings like a Queen or a small pocket pair while keeping the pot manageable if I miss.

I call 600. I have top pair (Qc) with a very weak 6‑kicker on Qs 2d Ac 4d — a hand that’s often behind any Ax and vulnerable to better Qx, but the turn bet is only ~1/3 of the pot. Pot = 1750, calling 600 requires ~25.5% equity (600 / (1750+600) ≈ 0.255). Flash opened wide preflop and has shown aggressive, mixed lines; his small flop c‑bet and this modest turn sizing are consistent with a range that contains both value (Ax, some Qx) and a meaningful number of bluffs. Folding here would be too tight given the price; raising would polarize and commit me with a weak kicker into a very deep, aggressive player. Calling keeps the pot controlled OOP, lets bluffs continue, and preserves stack depth to make a disciplined river decision. Plan: if Flash bets small/medium on the river (≈≤1/2 pot) I will call as a bluff‑catcher; if he fires a large, polarized river bet (>≈2/3 pot) I will fold unless the river brings a clear improvement for me (a Q for trips or another unlikely favourable card). If he checks back I’ll re‑evaluate the river and consider a small value bet on a safe card.

Board: Qs 2d Ac 4d 4c. I hold Qc6d — top pair with a very weak kicker. Flash (button) opened wide preflop, c‑bet small on the flop and bet the turn; he then checked back the paired river. That check-back is important information: on this river many of his value hands (any A, some Qx with decent kickers, any 4x or sets/full houses) either already beat me or turned into hands I can’t confidently bluff out. Betting here would commit chips OOP to get value mainly from worse Qx or thin calls, but it will also routinely be called by A‑x hands that beat me and can be raised by the small portion of his range that turned into a full house or a river shove. Pot control is the priority — I’m out of position, the kicker is weak, and the river pairing created more hands that beat me than fold to a bluff. I’ll check to take it to showdown and avoid getting exploited by a value raise or losing extra chips to marginal calls. If he bets a small thin value on the river I’ll fold to a large, polarized raise; if he checks back I accept the showdown with two pair (Qs and 4s).

I have missed both my flush and gutshot straight draws on the river. The board is Ace-high and I have been representing a strong hand (like an Ace) throughout the betting. However, Minni has called both the flop and turn bets, which strongly suggests she has a piece of the board, likely a Queen or an Ace. Since Minni is short-stacked with exactly 2350 chips remaining in a pot of 2350, she is very likely pot-committed and would call an all-in bet with any marginal pair. Bluffing here is low-frequency and risky against a player who has shown tenacity in previous hands. Checking back is the most prudent move to preserve my chip lead.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed pot with SB (Minni) having folded, leaving dead money from the SB blind. BB (Flash) has checked. My hand is Jd7s, offsuit but with a jack high and button position. This is a standard spot to raise wide for value + fold equity against a BB who may defend but not always. Pot is 150 with 50 dead; raising to 300 (3x the BB) offers good equity realization postflop if called due to position, and high chance to win dead money immediately. My stack of 5000 (50BB) supports this play. Folding is too tight, calling/limping is poor strategy.

I’m in the small blind with Jh7d facing a BTN open to 300 in a 3-handed game. J7o is a marginal, out-of-position hand and I don’t want to 3-bet as a bluff because I’ll be OOP to both players and risk bloating the pot with a dominated hand. Elon has been opening frequently, so folding is too tight. Calling 250 more (300 total committed) gives me reasonable pot odds to see a flop and realise equity while keeping the pot manageable. Plan: on a miss I’ll give up to significant pressure; continue only if I pair the J or pick up a good draw or favourable texture.

I am in the Big Blind holding Ace-Six offsuit. The Button (Elon) has raised to 300, and the Small Blind (Minni) has called. I need to commit 200 more chips to a pot that is currently 700. This gives me pot odds of 4.5 to 1. While Ace-Six offsuit isn't a premium hand, it performs well enough against a wide Button opening range and a Small Blind calling range to justify seeing a flop, especially since I close the action. Squeezing (raising) is an option but risky against two opponents with relatively short stacks (~45 BBs) who might jam over the top. Calling allows me to realize my equity and play a multi-way pot where I can potentially hit an Ace or a straight draw.

Flop Td Ad Kh vs Jh7d: I have no pair and only a very weak backdoor draw. I hold one diamond with two diamonds on the board (a double-run diamond flush would require both turn and river diamonds — ~5% by river) and I have 4 Q outs to make the Broadway straight by river (~16% by river). In a 3‑way pot versus the preflop raiser (Elon) and the big blind (Flash), leading or bluffing from the small blind with a raggy J7o is a losing line: it bloats the pot out of position and will get called or raised by many hands that currently beat me (Ax, Kx, broadways, tens). Plan: control the pot and see a cheap turn. Check and fold to significant pressure; only continue if I pair the J, hit a Q for the straight, or pick up a very favorable runout (diamond run or a board that cheapens future plays). If checked through I’ll reassess on the turn and get aggressive only with real improvement.

On a board of Td-Ad-Kh, I have top pair with a very weak kicker (Ace-Six). This board is extremely coordinated and connects heavily with the ranges of both the Button (Elon) and the Small Blind (Minni), who both called pre-flop. Any Broadway hand like QJ has already made a straight, and I am losing to any other Ace (AK, AQ, AJ, AT) as well as sets or two-pair combinations like KT. Since Minni has checked to me, the best course of action is to check as well and see how Elon, the pre-flop aggressor, reacts. Betting into two opponents on this texture with a weak-kicked top pair is unnecessary and risky.

Flop Td Ad Kh is coordinated with nut diamond flush draws, broadway straight draws (QJ), and hits top of my button raising range (Ax, Kx, broadways). Both opponents checked, indicating weakness or trapping, but in multiway pot with position, this is a standard c-bet spot to charge draws, get value from weaker Ax/KT, and take it down frequently vs missed hands. Jd7s is air but blocks QJ straight draw, has backdoor diamond draw, and plays well postflop in position. Pot 900, bet sizing 1/2 pot ~450 for good SPR control (leaves ~10:1), fold equity high given board and action. Checking back risks giving free cards to draws or allowing bluffs.

Call 450. The flop bet is small (450 into a 1350 pot) so I’m getting ~25% pot odds to continue (450/(1350+450) ≈ 25%). Holding Jh7d on Td Ad Kh I have ~7 clean outs to make a straight or pair (3 Jacks + 4 Queens) which gives me roughly a mid-to-high-20s percent chance to improve by showdown, plus a remote two-card diamond-run chance — overall my raw equity is around the break-even mark or a bit better versus a typical c-bet range. Elon has been opening a lot and this sizing looks like a standard continuation bet rather than a heavy polarization; folding here is too nitty, and raising as a bluff OOP into two players (Flash is behind with a very deep stack) is a losing line that bloats the pot with a weak hand. Plan: call to realize equity and keep the pot manageable; if I miss the turn and Elon applies significant pressure I’ll fold, but if I hit a Jack/Queen or pick up a favorable diamond/board texture I’ll switch to a more aggressive line.

I have top pair on a very coordinated and wet board (Td Ad Kh). Elon, the pre-flop aggressor, has bet 450 into two players, and Minni has called. While my kicker (6) is very weak and I am likely outkicked by other Aces in their ranges (AK, AQ, AJ, AT) or even losing to straights (QJ) and two-pairs (KT), the pot odds are currently 4:1 (450 to call into a total pot of 1800 + 450). Closing the action on the flop with top pair at these odds makes a call the standard play, though I must proceed with extreme caution on future streets if the betting continues.

Check. I missed the turn — Jh7d on Td Ad Kh 3h gives me only 7 outs to improve (3 Jacks + 4 Queens) for ~15% to hit by the river and no realistic flush possibility anymore. Pot is 2,250 and my stack is 3,950. Betting as a bluff OOP into two players (one was the preflop aggressor who c‑bet the flop and one cold‑called) is a losing line: it bloats the pot, will get called by many hands that currently beat me (A/K/T, pairs, broadway combos), and will be difficult to fold if raised. My preflop/flop plan was to realize equity; I’ll stick to pot control here. Check and give up to meaningful pressure on the river — only continue if I pair the J, hit a Q for Broadway, or see a very cheap bet that the pot odds justify calling.

On this extremely coordinated board (Td-Ad-Kh-3h), my top pair with a six kicker remains a marginal hand. The flop action (Elon betting into two players and Minni calling) indicates that one or both of them likely hold a piece of this board, potentially an Ace with a better kicker, two pair, or even a made straight (QJ). Since Minni has checked the turn, the most prudent action is to check as well. This allows me to see how Elon reacts and prevents me from bloating a pot where I am likely outkicked or already drawing thin. If Elon bets and there is a call or raise ahead of me, I will likely have to fold.

Turn board 3h is a low brick that doesn't significantly change the draw structure (diamonds still live, heart flush draw introduced but low probability). Multiway pot after my flop c-bet was called by both opponents who now check again, showing continued weakness or traps, but given history of passive play in spots (e.g., frequent checks), likely weak Ax, mid-pairs, or missed draws. Jd7s has zero equity (no pair, no draws; gutshot dead with board texture, Jd blocks some straight draws but irrelevant now). Position allows checking back safely: pot control with air, avoid bloating vs calling stations/calling ranges, realize negligible showdown value cheaply, induce bluffs or thin value on river if improved unimproved. Betting bluffs multiway into scary board risks calls from Ax/Kx/top pair while denying myself free showdown; SPR ~2:1 post-check favorable for river play. Checking optimal to balance image after aggressive pre/flop lines, exploits opponents' check tendencies seen in prior hands (e.g., Hand 34, 47, etc.).

Final board Td Ad Kh 3h 5d. I hold Jh7d — no pair and I only have one diamond (board diamonds are Td, Ad, 5d = 3 diamonds), so I do NOT have a flush. I called the flop to realize equity and controlled the pot on the turn; the river 5d didn’t improve me to a pair or straight. Pot is 2,250 and my stack is 3,950. Betting here for thin value is a losing line: J-high beats almost nothing and a value bet will be called by almost any pair (T/K/A) or stronger broadway, while bluffing faces two opponents who both checked the turn and river — fold equity is low in a 3‑way spot. A small probe bet would need a high fold frequency to be profitable; a larger bet risks a significant portion of my stack to a fold or a raise from a value hand. Best long‑term line is pot control: check and preserve chips. If either opponent then bets, I’ll fold to meaningful pressure with no pair and negligible showdown equity.

On the river, the board is Td-Ad-Kh-3h-5d. I have top pair with an Ace, but my kicker (six) is very weak. The flop action was aggressive, but the turn checked around, suggesting that either everyone is marginal or someone is trapping. Since Minni has checked to me, checking is the best play to realize my showdown value. Betting would likely only get called by better Aces or hands that beat me, and checking allows me to see what Elon does behind me. If Elon bets, I will have to evaluate based on his sizing, but for now, checking is the most prudent move.

River 5d completes the nut diamond flush draw (AdTd5d on board means any diamond completes a strong flush, e.g., KdQd nuts or weaker), adds straight draw outs minimally (e.g., 64 for wheel unlikely), and pairs nothing relevant. Board extremely coordinated/scary: A-high flush possible, broadway draws live pre-river. My Jd7s is pure air—no pair, busted backdoor equity (Jd gave minor diamond blocker but irrelevant), jack-high no showdown. Multiway after my flop cbet called by both (pot 2250 now), opponents check river again signaling weakness (no strong hands betting out, unlikely nuts check multiway without value bet attempt given pot size ~10BB). Bluffing suicidal: low fold equity vs. 2 opponents who defended flop (Ax, Kx, pairs, draws now hit or showdown value), risks full stack commitment into calling ranges/stations (history: frequent check-calls, e.g., Hands 33,44,48). Deep SPR pre-river check preserved position; now check back exploits passive tendencies (e.g., Hands 7,38,47,50 checkdown rivers unimproved), pot controls air, induces possible thin value if opponent improves to 2pr/weak flush or realizes equity cheaply at SDV. Betting disrupts balanced Cbet-check line, overbluffs vs. history of pot control. Optimal: check, chop pots when missed.

I’m on the button with 5♠2♠ — a very weak but suited holding. It’s folded to me and the pot is 150 (SB 50, BB 100). Limping (call 100) is passive and invites the blinds to see a cheap flop; a min-raise to 200 is too small and will also attract too many callers. I want fold equity and to leverage position, so I’ll open to a standard 3x BB sizing (300). This sizing pressures the blinds while keeping risk reasonable with my ~39 BB stack. Plan: fold to any sizable 3-bet (this hand doesn’t play well against strong ranges); if called I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and give up when clearly disadvantaged.

I am in the Small Blind with King-Trey offsuit. The Button (Minni) has raised to 300. While I have a massive chip lead over the other two players, King-Trey offsuit is a very weak hand to play out of position against a raise. Given that both opponents are relatively short-stacked (under 45 BB), I'd prefer to wait for better cards or better spots to apply pressure rather than defending with a marginal hand.

Preflop in BB facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds. My hand Qc8h is offsuit, disconnected, and queen-high. Standard to fold marginal offsuit broadway hands OOP vs button open range. Pot odds are 450 to call 200 (roughly 2.25:1, needing ~31% equity), but Q8o has around 30% raw equity vs a wide button range (~40-50%), and postflop playability is poor OOP with no suitedness or connectivity. Minni's history shows frequent raises but defending requires better. Folding preserves stack for better spots.

I am on the button with King-Trey offsuit. While King-high has some value in three-handed play, this specific hand is quite weak (K3o). Both Elon and Minni have relatively short stacks (approximately 40 big blinds each) and have shown significant pre-flop aggression in previous hands. Raising here might invite a shove from these shorter stacks, forcing me to fold and lose chips unnecessarily. Given my massive stack lead, I can afford to wait for a more favorable hand to pressure my opponents.

Q5o is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability and dominated by any Qx or pair from BB. Facing a fold from Button, it's heads-up OOP vs Minni. Pot odds of 3:1 aren't worth entering with trash preflop, risking tough postflop spots. Standard fold to preserve stack for better opportunities.

Preflop on the button with J2o in a 3-handed pot. While button position allows for wider ranges in short-handed play, J2 offsuit is a very weak hand with poor playability, low equity against likely calling or raising ranges from blinds, especially given Flash's massive stack and aggressive history of raising pots, and Minni's frequent involvement. Folding conserves my 41BB stack for better spots to maximize long-term EV over 150 hands.

Heads-up in the small blind with 9♥3♥. The pot is 150 and it costs me 50 more to complete, which would make my total committed 100. That price is very cheap (50 into 150 → ~4:1; I only need ~20% equity). 9-3 suited has some playability (backdoor heart flush, occasional pair/straight potential) and I’ll have postflop position (in heads-up the small blind is the button and acts last after the flop), which makes a cheap call profitable more often than a blind squeeze. Flash (BB) is deep and aggressive — raising here with a weak hand risks getting 3-bet or turned into a big pot out of line. Folding is too tight given the pot odds. Plan: call the 50 (100 total committed), see the flop and proceed based on texture — continue only with real equity (pair, hearts, strong draws), otherwise give up to heavy aggression.
I am in the big blind with Q3o. Minni has limped from the small blind. Since there is no raise, I will take the free flop and check.

Flop completely misses 9♥3♥. Board 5♣6♣10♣ — no club in my hand, no pair, only a backdoor heart flush and a very remote runner-runner straight. Flash checked; with essentially zero realized equity and a very deep, aggressive opponent who can punish a c-bet, I should not build the pot. Check to control the pot and plan to fold to meaningful aggression. If the turn brings a heart or gives me real equity I can reevaluate, otherwise give it up.

I am in the big blind with Q3o on a mono-tone club board (5c 6c Tc). I do not have a club. The pot is very small (200 chips) and Minni has checked to me. There is no reason to bluff here as I have no equity if called, and I have mediocre showdown value as Queen-high. I will check and see if I can improve on the turn or get to a cheap showdown.

Turn paired my 3 (I hold 9♥3♥) giving me a weak bottom pair on a 5♣-6♣-10♣-3♦ board. Flash checked flop and checked turn, which shows either a missed club-heavy range, weak overcards, or an intention to check-call — not an obviously strong hand. This is a classic pot‑control spot: my hand has some showdown value but is vulnerable to any pair/overpair, clubs, or pockets that improved. Betting here would be thin value and creates three‑bet/raise pressure from a deep, aggressive opponent; I prefer to keep the pot small and see the river. Action: check and plan to call a small river bet (or a bet that’s under ~1/3–1/2 pot) but fold to large aggression. Checking preserves fold equity and keeps marginal calls manageable while still allowing me to extract value if he gives up.

On the turn, the 3d gives me bottom pair. In a limped pot with Minni (Small Blind), she has checked twice. While bottom pair is weak, it has some showdown value against her checking range. There is little value in betting as I likely won't get called by worse, and a bet might induce a check-raise. Checking behind allows me to keep the pot small and potentially realize my equity or bluff-catch on the river if she leads.

River is 5♣6♣T♣3♦2♣; I hold 9♥3♥ — only bottom pair (trey) with a weak kicker. Flash checked flop and turn which reduces (but doesn’t eliminate) the probability he has a club flush or a big made hand; given his demonstrated aggression in previous hands, he tends to bet value rather than trap very often. There are many hands that beat me (any club, tens, higher pairs, two‑pair/trips), and a river bet here is thin value — a bluff risks a raise from a better hand and I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal holding. Pot is small (200) relative to my stack. Best play is to check to showdown and only call a small river bet; fold to large raises. Action: check.

The board has four clubs and I have a pair of threes. In this limped pot, Minni has checked on every street, suggesting a very weak range. While any club beats me, my pair of threes has some showdown value against her non-club air. There is no reason to bet here, as I'm unlikely to be called by worse, and a bet won't necessarily fold out a better hand if she has a small club or a better pair she's unwilling to let go of in a tiny pot. I will check and take the showdown.

I have A9s on the button with two players left to act (SB Flash is very deep/aggressive; BB Elon is shortish ~40 BB and likely to defend). This is a good spot to open — A9s plays well postflop in position and has fold equity preflop. I’ll raise to a standard 3x (300) to apply pressure, reduce multiway callers, and either take the pot now or go heads-up in position. The size (300) matches the table’s typical open and keeps the pot manageable vs a potential 3-bet from Flash or an isolated shove from Elon; I will re-evaluate if I face a large 3-bet/shove.

I am in the Small Blind with Six of Spades and Seven of Hearts. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. While 67 offsuit is a connector, it is a very weak hand to play out of position, especially against a short stack (Minni has about 37.5 BB left) who is likely to be aggressive. Folding here is the most disciplined play to avoid being put in a difficult spot post-flop with low equity.

In the big blind facing a button raise to 300 after SB fold, my ATo offsuit has good equity against a wide button opening range (likely 30-40% of hands). Aces are strong blockers, and this spot is profitable to defend. Folding is too tight, calling OOP risks playing a marginal ace postflop multiway potentially. 3-betting to 900 (3x the raise) is standard sizing here with 40BB stacks, isolates the button, builds the pot with equity advantage, and applies pressure on their wide range. Minni's history shows aggression but also folds to 3bets in similar spots. Willing to fold to a 4bet shove given stacks.

Elon 3-bet to 900 after my open to 300. I have A♥9♥ on the button (stack 3750 vs his ~3200 effective). A9s is a strong hand to play in position against a shortish 3-bettor: good equity and post-flop playability. Calling 600 (to make my total 900) keeps the pot manageable, gives me position, and gets roughly ~32% pot-odds (600 into a 1250 pot) — which A9s should clear vs a reasonably wide 3-bet range. I don’t want to 4-bet-bluff or bloat the pot with a marginal hand here; if Elon jams over my call the price to call would be poor and I’ll fold. Postflop I’ll proceed aggressively with top pair or strong draws and control the pot or fold vs heavy action on dry boards.

Preflop 3bet with ATo is standard as BB vs late position open, isolating with ace blocker and playability. Flop is 666 rainbow, an extremely dry paired board where my ace-high has zero showdown value and no draws. As OOP 3bet pot OOP first to act, villain's calling range (decent after flatting 3bet) includes many 6x combos (A6,K6,Q6,J6,T6,96,86,76,65, plus 66 for quads), dominating any bluff catcher like ace-high. No flush/straight draws complete meaningfully. Betting risks raising/folding equity loss with air while getting called by worse rarely (overpairs unlikely to flat pre and stay in). Checking folds out villain's air bluffs but protects against their value; pot-committal stacks (~3200 into 1850) but no equity to realize value. Plan: check-fold to any bet, no hero calls needed.

Flop: 6c 6d 6h — board is trip sixes. I hold A♥9♥ with no pair and my opponent (Elon) 3‑bet preflop, so his range is relatively strong and can include Ax/broadways and the one remaining 6 (which would be quads). Elon checked, giving me the opportunity to either bluff or pot‑control. With no real equity and facing a short, strong 3‑bettor, betting here just bloats a pot I’m likely behind in or gets raised/jammed by a hand that crushes me. Best line is pot control: check and be prepared to fold to meaningful aggression. If he checks back I can reassess on the turn (and extract value if I pair up).

Preflop 3bet with ATo OOP was standard isolation vs button open. Flop 666 rainbow checked through by both, preserving my air without risking a fold to a bet from her value-heavy range (many 6x, sets). Turn 5h adds a weak heart flush draw (one prior heart on flop) and open-ended straight draw possibilities (e.g., 7-high), but my AdTc has zero equity: no pair, no flush draw (wrong suits), no straight draw, ace-high no showdown. Her flatting range pre (after my 3bet) favors Ax suited, pairs 77+, suited connectors/broadways. Checking flop OOP was correct to pot control; now first to act on turn, betting as a bluff is disastrous OOP into checked board: low fold equity vs her value (any 6 crushes me, overpairs/sets stay), stacks ~3200 effective into 1850 pot mean any bet gets shoved on, losing my stack with 0% equity. Check preserves stack, induces bluffs (her air/overpairs might bet), and allows cheap fold to her value bet. Plan: check, fold to bet. Long-term +EV to avoid spew.

The flop (6c 6d 6h) is extremely hostile to my calling range — Elon 3‑bet preflop, and many hands in that 3‑bet range (pocket pairs, big pairs) now have full houses or quads. I hold A♥9♥ with no pair and only a one‑card nut‑heart draw on the river (four hearts seen including my two, ~9 outs ≈ 19% to hit). Betting the turn would bloat a pot I'm almost certainly behind in and can easily induce a shove I would have to fold to; calling or raising would be poor risk/reward. Best line is pot‑control: check and see if Elon shows weakness. If he bets large I will fold; if he checks back I get a free river to try to realize my ~19% equity or bluff if the river presents a good story. Preserve my stack and re-evaluate on the river.

River board 6c6d6h5h4s completes some straight draws (e.g., 73 for 7-high straight) and backdoor flush draws, but my AdTc remains pure ace-high bluff catcher with no pair, no completed draw, and minimal showdown value against Minni's 3bet-flatting range (heavy on 6x combos for full house/trips, Ax suited, sets like 66 for quads, overpairs that survived checks). Preflop 3bet isolation correct; checked through flop/turn pot control correct OOP with air on dry-then-drawy board to avoid spew. Now OOP first to act on river, SPR ~1.7 into 1850 pot with 3200 effective stacks. Bluffing disastrous: low fold equity vs her capped strong range (value jams/calls any bet sizing), risks full stack loss with 0 equity. Checking optimal: induces bluffs/mistakes from her air/overpairs/A-high worse, realizes equity passively, controls pot, easy fold to value bet (no hero call needed vs her range). Long-term EV positive to check-fold.

Board is disastrously bad for my hand: 6c 6d 6h 5h 4s and I hold A♥9♥ — no pair and I missed the backdoor heart flush. Elon 3‑betted preflop, so his range is weighted to hands that frequently make a full house (any 5 or 4, or any pocket pair) or even quads. He checked flop, turn and just checked the river — that can be either weakness or a deceptive trap, but given the preflop action his calling/checking range still contains many hands that will happily call a river bluff. Betting here would bloat a pot I’m almost certainly behind in and would get called by lots of better hands; a shove or check‑raise from him would be disastrous. With ~3150 behind and a 1850 pot, the risk/reward for a bluff is poor. Best play is pot control: check and fold to any meaningful aggression. I’ll check and preserve my stack for better spots.

I am on the button with Queen-Deuce offsuit. This is a very weak starting hand, even in a three-handed game. Given that I have a massive chip lead over the other two players (Elon with 50 BBs and Minni with 30.5 BBs), I do not need to play marginal or weak hands. Minni has shown significant aggression in previous hands, so opening with trash like Q2o is likely to lead to a difficult post-flop situation or a loss to a 3-bet. I will fold and wait for a better hand to utilize my stack advantage.

Preflop in SB with 3d8h offsuit, a very weak hand with poor playability, no high card value, limited straight or flush potential. Flash has folded, heads-up vs Minni in BB. Completing the SB would be too loose given the hand strength; better to fold and preserve chips for better spots. Standard fold to avoid difficult postflop situations OOP with trash.

3-handed on the button with AKo, a premium hand. SB limped (weakness), BB checked (no strength shown). Pot is small (~150-250), my stack 5000 allows aggression. Raise to isolate the limper, build the pot with top-tier hand, take initiative preflop. Standard sizing around 3x BB in this spot given history of 300 raises. Opponents loose/passive callers historically, good for value.

I’m in the small blind with 4♠6♦ facing a standard button open to 300. Pot is 450 and I must call 250. This hand is weak, has poor playability and will be dominated by most of the button’s opening range. I’m out of position for the entire hand and the big blind still acts behind me, so calling would commit chips for marginal equity (I’d need ~36% to break even versus the current pot) and raising to 500 would be an unnecessary bluff with very little fold equity and too much risk. With ~31.5 BB effective, preserve the stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

I am in the Big Blind with Td 9c. Elon on the Button raised to 300. Minni in the Small Blind folded. I need to call 200 more into a pot that will be 650. This gives me very good pot odds (about 2.25 to 1). T9o is a strong enough hand to defend against a standard button open, especially given the stack sizes and the price. I will call to see a flop.

I have T-high on a board of Kd-5s-4h after calling a pre-flop raise from the Button. I have completely missed the flop and have no significant draws. I am first to act and should check to the pre-flop aggressor to see how they react. I do not have enough equity to lead out or check-call a standard bet.

TPTK with AsKh on Kd5s4h flop, a strong hand that likely leads the range. Flash called a button raise OOP from BB and checked flop, showing weakness. Board has straight draws (e.g., 36o, 23o for wheel) and pair + draw possibilities, so c-bet for value and protection. Pot 650, ~60% pot sizing (400) balances value from worse pairs/draws while denying equity to floats/draws. My stack 4700 allows aggression; Flash deep but passive history in BB spots.

I defended the big blind with T9o and the flop came K-5-4 rainbow, which completely missed my hand. Elon, the pre-flop aggressor, has followed up with a continuation bet of 400 into a 650 pot. With no pair, no draw, and very little back-door equity, I cannot justify a call or a raise. The best play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

5♣2♥ is the bottom of the barrel — almost never good. There are two opponents (SB Flash is deep and capable of aggression, BB Elon has a medium stack), so calling 100 would put me in a multiway pot out of position with a hand that has negligible playability and is often dominated. A light raise to steal is a bluff I don’t want to risk here against these stacks and tendencies. My stack (3,150) is healthy enough to wait for real spots. Fold and conserve chips for a better position/hand.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Seven offsuit. Since the Button has folded, it is just me and the Big Blind. Ace-high is a strong hand in a blind vs blind situation. I will raise to 300 to put pressure on Elon and take control of the pot.

5h3d is a complete trash hand: offsuit, low, disconnected cards with poor playability out of position against Flash's aggressive open-raise from the SB. Pot odds are about 3:1 (calling 200 into 400), but equity realization OOP vs a deep-stacked opponent who frequently continuation bets will be low. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

In Hand #70, I am on the button with 5d 3s. This is a very weak hand. While I am deep-stacked (31,600 chips) and have the position, my hand has very little potential. Given the stack sizes of Elon (5,200) and Minni (3,050), they are relatively short-stacked and may be looking for opportunities to shove or play aggressively pre-flop. Folding this hand is the standard and most prudent play to avoid unnecessary risk with a bottom-tier starting hand.

6c2s is one of the worst possible starting hands: offsuit, unconnected, low cards with minimal straight/flush potential and dominated in most scenarios. In SB facing BB after Button fold, completing the 50 into 150 pot (3:1 implied odds) would leave me OOP postflop with trash. No reason to defend or raise this speculative hand against Minni's BB range. Folding preserves stack for better spots.

3-handed on the button with T8 suited connectors, an excellent speculative hand with great playability, position, and implied odds against deep stacks. Pot is 150 with 100 to call, likely a standard limp-check scenario. Raising steals the blinds frequently or builds a pot where I have position OOP opponents. Minni (SB) has been call-y but folds to aggression; Flash (BB) is deep and aggressive but can fold weak hands. Standard button raise size of 3x BB to 300 total applies pressure while keeping pot manageable. Folding too tight preflop on button 3-handed; calling is fine but raising maximizes EV through fold equity and initiative.

Short‑handed (3 players) the Button’s 3x open is wide — Kc9d is marginal but has good playability in this spot. Folding is too tight; a min‑raise to 500 is a weak isolation that will likely get called or 3‑bet by the very deep Big Blind. I’ll defend by calling the extra 250 (total 300) to keep the pot controlled and see a flop. If Flash 3‑bets large I’ll fold given my hand and stack depth; if we see a flop I’ll proceed cautiously as first to act, looking to realize equity or fold to heavy pressure on dry boards, and to extract value when I hit top pair or strong draws.

I am in the Big Blind with a pocket pair of Threes (3h 3c). The button (Elon) raised to 300, and the Small Blind (Minni) called. I only need to call 200 more to see a flop in a pot that will be 900. Both of my opponents are extremely short-stacked (4900 and 2900 respectively), which means if I hit a set, I am very likely to get their entire stacks. This is a classic 'set mining' opportunity where the pot odds and implied odds make calling the most profitable play.

I called preflop to see a flop in a 3‑handed spot; on 6♦ 7♣ J♥ with K♣9♦ I have no pair and only a weak backdoor club/straight possibility. This is a multiway, moderately large pot (900) and both opponents’ ranges contain lots of Jx, pairs and strong overcards that connect with this board. Bluffing or raising here has very limited fold equity and bad equity if called. I’ll check to control the pot and avoid committing chips with a marginal hand. Plan: if faced with a small c‑bet (~≤300) I can call to see a cheap turn; fold to sizable bets (≥~600) and re‑evaluate only if I pair my K or pick up a real draw.

I have an underpair (pocket threes) on a board of Jack-Seven-Six. In a three-way pot after defending the big blind, this hand has very little value unless I improve to a set, which is unlikely now that the flop has dealt. Minni has checked, and Elon, the pre-flop aggressor, is behind me. I will check and likely fold to any significant betting action, as my hand is easily beaten by any Jack, Seven, Six, or higher pocket pair.

Preflop, I raised with T8c suited connectors from the button in a 3-handed pot, a strong speculative hand with position and multiway potential. Flop 6d-7c-Jh is a decent board for my range: hits some straight draws, pairs my 8/T as gutshot (needing a 9 for 6-7-8-9-T), backdoor club flush draw (two clubs in hand, 7c on board), and overcard potential. Rainbow board reduces flush threats. Both opponents checked, which is weak especially from Flash in BB after calling a raise—likely no J or strong pairs, often air, weak pairs, or draws. As preflop raiser with position, this is a standard c-bet spot to charge draws (like 5-8, 8-9, 5-9), take initiative, protect equity, and deny equity to their floats. Pot 900, bet 1/3 (~300) balances value (possible 88, T7s in range thin value) and bluffs (this hand fits as semi-bluff with 6+ outs to straight + backdoors ~20% equity). Their stacks: Minni 29BB may fold medium strength, Flash deep can call lighter but checks suggest weakness. Checking risks giving free cards to their draws/overcards OOP. Bet sizing 300 total to pot keeps SPR manageable (~10:1 post-bet), allows fold equity while inducing calls from worse.

I checked the flop to control the pot after calling preflop. Elon’s small c‑bet (300 into a 900 preflop pot → pot is 1200) gives me ~20% pot odds to call, which is a cheap price to realize equity with Kc9d (backdoor club and pair outs). This is a multiway pot with limited fold equity, so raising is unnecessary and folding to a small c‑bet is overly nitty. Plan: call the 300 to see a turn; fold to a large turn bet (~≥600) if I don’t improve, but if I pair my K or pick up a real draw I’ll proceed aggressively for value or to pressure.

I have pocket threes on a 6-7-J rainbow board. In this three-way pot, Elon (the pre-flop aggressor) has placed a continuation bet of 300, and Minni has called. My pocket pair is an underpair to the entire board, and Minni's call signifies she likely has a piece of the board or a drawing hand that beats mine. I am only drawing to two remaining threes in the deck (roughly a 4% chance on the turn), and the pot odds (6:1) do not justify a call given the low probability of improvement and the fact that I am facing two opponents. Folding is the most disciplined and mathematically sound play.

I have K♣9♦ on 6♦-7♣-J♥-4♣. I called the small flop c‑bet to realize equity, but the turn gives me nothing — no pair, no realistic straight and no flush (I have one club and there are only two clubs on board, so with one card to come I cannot make a 5‑card flush). My clean outs are 3 kings + 3 nines = 6 outs ≈ 6/46 ≈ 13% to improve on the river.

Pot control OOP is the priority. The pot is 1,500 and I have no reason to bloat it with K‑high; betting as a bluff has poor fold equity against Elon's range (he opened preflop and c‑bet the flop, so he still has many Jx/6x/7x and strong pairs that will call). I’ll check to keep the pot manageable and either see a free river or induce a bluff that I can fold/call depending on sizing. Plan: if Elon fires a small turn bet (~≤225 chips) I may call for the pot odds and because his range contains bluffs; fold to larger turn barrels (anything materially bigger than ~225 should be a fold given my ~13% to improve). If he checks back, I go to the river and will value bet any clean pair (K) or bluff selectively if the river card and his line allow it.

Action: check to control the pot and follow the above fold/call thresholds on any turn bet.

Heads-up on turn after Flash folded flop. Board: 6d-7c-Jh-4c rainbow except two clubs. My Tc8c: strong combo draw with nut flush draw (9 outs), gutshot to 4-5-6-7-8 straight (4 outs, total ~12 clean outs or 45%+ equity). Preflop raise + flop c-bet called by Minni (who checked preflop/flop/turn), her range weakened: weak Jx, 6x/7x, stubborn draws (89o/s, T9s, 5x for str), overcards/floats unlikely to improve much on 4c. Continued checks suggest no strong hands. Semi-bluff bet exploits fold equity against her capped range (rare sets/top pair good given check/call flop), denies equity to her draws (e.g., 58, 89), builds pot for when I hit. Position allows control. Pot 1500, Minni 2600 eff. Bet 1000 (~2/3 pot) balances value from weaker draws/weak pairs, fold equity to air, sets up river shove (2600 into ~3500 SPR ~1.3:1, profitable with equity/pos). Checking risks free river to her (e.g., 5,A,9,K completing), folds too equity. Aggressive line consistent with preflop/flop.

I called preflop and the small flop c‑bet to realize equity, with the explicit plan to fold to a large turn barrel — that just arrived. Pot math: there were ~6 clean outs (3 Ks + 3 9s) ≈ 6/46 ≈ 13% equity to pair on the river. Elon’s turn bet (1000 into the 1500 pot → pot would be 3500 if I call) means I must have ~28.6% equity to break even (1000/3500), so a call is strongly -EV. His line (open, flop c‑bet, now a big turn barrel) is consistent with value (Jx, 6x/7x, two‑pair/sets/overpairs), and I have almost no fold equity to a raise. Raising as a bluff is too risky and likely to be called or reshoved. Conclusion: fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

Button with K♠9♦ (26bb effective). Pot is 150 and it’s only 100 to see the flop, but this hand is worth opening for value and fold equity from the blinds. A standard 3xBB-ish open to 300 (total) puts pressure on the SB/BB, preserves stack depth (leaves ~23bb behind) and avoids leaving the initiative to the blinds with a tiny raise. Plan: fold to a large 3-bet (we’re dominated too often vs a polarized 3-bet), but if we get one or two callers we’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and control the pot on connected/overcard boards. If both fold we take down the 150 pot. Raise size chosen to maximize steal equity without committing too many chips with a marginal hand.

I have pocket eights in the Small Blind. Minni has raised to 300 from the button, but her remaining stack is very short, only 2000 chips behind. Elon in the Big Blind has 6000. Pocket eights are a strong hand three-handed. Given Minni's short stack and the strength of my hand, I want to isolate or potentially just get the money in against her. A 3-bet here is standard to prevent Elon from seeing a cheap flop and to put pressure on Minni. I will raise to 1000, which effectively commits a large portion of Minni's stack if she decides to continue.

32o is one of the worst hands in poker, completely uncoordinated and low. Facing a button open from Minni and a 3-bet from aggressive SB Flash, my hand has negligible equity. Pot odds are about 1.55:1 (1400 pot to 900 call), but even against wide ranges, 32o rarely wins. Folding preserves my 6000 stack for premium spots in a long session (72/150 hands). No reason to speculate.

I opened to 300 from the button with K♠9♦ to steal and get to see flops in position. Flash (SB) 3-bet to 1000 — a fairly large, polarized 3-bet that will be heavy in value (AK/AQ/TT+) with some bluffs. Pot is 1400 and it’s 700 to call, so I need ~33% equity to justify a call. K9o is frequently dominated by a value-heavy 3-bet range and I don’t want to 4-bet/commit with such a marginal holding with only ~23bb effective. Follow my preflop plan to fold to a large 3-bet and preserve chips for cleaner spots in position.

I am on the button with King-Queen of Spades, which is a very strong hand in a three-handed game. My opponents are relatively short-stacked, with Minni having only 22 big blinds and Elon having about 60 big blinds. I will open for a standard raise of 300 to build the pot and exert pressure on the blinds. If either player shoves, I have a strong enough hand to consider calling, especially against Minni's short stack.

Preflop in SB with Ac7h facing a button raise to 3BB from aggressive player Flash. A7s is a marginal hand OOP; it plays poorly post-flop without a strong ace or good connectivity. Minni is short-stacked in BB and may get involved, making multi-way pots worse. Pot odds are okay for a call (250 into 700 effective), but position and hand realization issues make folding the disciplined play to preserve stack for better spots over 150 hands.

Facing a button open to 300 with 6♦7♦ in the big blind (stack 2200). This is a playable suited connector against a likely wide button raising range. Pot is 450 and I must add 200 (300 total committed) — pot odds ~31%, which justifies defending. With only 22bb effective, I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position with a small 3-bet that invites a bigger response. I’ll call to realize equity and play the flop; proceed aggressively if I hit (pair/strong draws), otherwise control the pot and fold to large pressure OOP.

Dry, low board (2♥ 3♦ 8♥). With 6♦7♦ I have no pair and only modest equity — about 6 immediate outs to pair (≈24% to hit by river) and tiny runner‑runner diamond/straight chances. I defended preflop to realize equity, but out of position vs a deep, aggressive button I don’t want to bloat the pot with a marginal hand. I’ll check to control the pot, take a free turn, and only continue if I improve or if the opponent gives correct pot odds with a small c‑bet; fold to large sustained pressure OOP.

On a board of 2h-3d-8h, I have two overcards and a backdoor flush draw with KsQs. The board is quite dry but has some straight and flush potential. Minni checked, and as the pre-flop aggressor, this is a standard spot to fire a continuation bet. A bet of about half the pot should be enough to take it down or charge draws/marginal hands. Given Minni's short stack (2000 remaining), I want to bet enough to put pressure on her without committing myself if she check-raises all-in.

Flash c-bet 350 into the ~650 pre‑bet pot (pot is 1000 now). Calling 350 to see the turn requires roughly 26% equity (350 / 1350 ≈ 25.9%). With 6♦7♦ on 2♥3♦8♥ I have ~24% to pair by the river plus a few percent of backdoor diamond/runner‑runner straight equity — roughly ~28–30% vs a wide c‑betting range. I defended preflop to realize equity and, out of position, I don’t want to bloat the pot or bluff‑raise with a marginal hand that has limited fold equity. Calling keeps the pot manageable, lets me realize my equity, and preserves stack to fold to heavy turn aggression if I miss. Plan: call now; if I pair or pick up a diamond on the turn I’ll proceed more aggressively, otherwise I’ll fold to large continued pressure.

I’m on the turn with 6♦7♦ on 2♥3♦8♥8♠ — I have no pair and only 6 outs to pair on the river (~13% chance). The pot is 1350 and my stack is 1650. Flash is a wide/aggressive raiser and his flop c‑bet suggests many hands that still crush me (8x, overpairs, strong pairs) or will barrel as a bluff — but my equity is too low to bet for fold equity or to bloat the pot out of position. Best line is pot control: check the turn, see the river for my one-card equity shot, then fold to a sizable bet if I miss or value/call/raise narrowly if I hit.

The board has paired on the turn with the 8s, which is generally a good card for the pre-flop aggressor. However, Minni is playing a very short stack (only 1650 remaining). My King-Queen high still has some showdown value against her range, which includes many draws (like heart draws or gutshots). If I bet now, I'm almost certainly committing her to the pot if she has any piece of the board or a draw, and I might get check-raised all-in. By checking back, I control the size of the pot, keep her bluffs in, and can potentially see a free river to improve to a pair. Given her stack size and the board texture, checking behind is a safe way to realize equity.

Board: 2♥ 3♦ 8♥ 8♠ A♦ — I hold 6♦7♦ and missed. Pot is 1,350 and my stack is 1,650. Flash is a wide/aggressive opener who c‑bet the flop and then checked the turn. I have no pair, no flush and no realistic river outs. Betting here would be a pure bluff that must achieve a fairly high fold frequency to be +EV: an all‑in would need ~55% folds, a half‑pot ~33%. Given his line (flop bet, turn check) and the scary A♦ on the river that hits a lot of his range, I don’t expect him to fold enough. Conserving stack for better spots and avoiding a marginal bluff OOP is best. I will check and fold to any meaningful river bet; if he checks back I accept the likely loss at showdown rather than risking a large bluff.

On this river, an Ace appears, which is a significant scare card. As the pre-flop aggressor, this card theoretically favors my range. However, Minni is playing a very short stack (1650 chips remaining in a 1350 chip pot). My King-Queen high has almost no showdown value against any pair (22-77), which she could easily have called the flop with. While a bluff might force a fold from those small pairs, short-stacked players are often more inclined to call with any piece of the board. Given that she checked the river, I will check behind. I might win against missed heart draws (like 6h7h or 9hTh), and checking avoids the risk of being check-raised or called by a better hand that won't fold for her remaining chips.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed pot with 5d8h offsuit, a weak hand with poor playability and low equity against calling or 3-bet ranges. Position is good, but 58o is below standard opening range even 3-handed (prefers pairs, suited connectors, broadways, suited aces). Minni short at 1600 (~16BB) may shove wide from SB, Flash deep in BB. Limping is poor strategy; raising risks too much for marginal hand; folding preserves stack for better spots in high-volume game.

I’m on the button (small blind) heads-up with Jc2h — a weak hand but I have postflop position and only ~16BB effective. Limping/calling lets Flash see a cheap flop and gives up fold equity; shoving is too high-variance with this junk. I want to apply pressure to steal the pot while keeping enough chips behind to play postflop if called. A standard 3x-ish open to 300 gives good fold equity vs a wide BB defending range while preserving maneuverability. If called I’ll use my positional advantage to c-bet or fold to strong resistance; if he folds I win the 150 pot outright.

I am in the big blind with Q7 offsuit. The small blind (Minni) has raised to 300, leaving her with only 1,050 chips behind. This is a very small stack relative to the blinds and the pot. While Q7 is not a premium hand, I only need to call 200 more into a pot of 400. However, given Minni's extremely short stack, she is likely committed to the hand if I call and she hits anything, or she might just be shoving any two cards soon. At this stack depth, Q7o is a marginal defend, but since it's a battle of the blinds and the price is good, I will call to see a flop, though I must be wary of her limited remaining chips.

I opened preflop from the button with a very wide steal and got called in the big blind. Flop is 6d 8c 5c — I have Jc2h: no pair, but a club blocker (Jc) and the initiative. Flash’s calling range is wide and contains many missed hands (A/K/Q highs, small pairs, some suited hands) that will fold to pressure. I want to use position and fold equity to take the pot now while keeping the pot manageable if called. A half-pot c-bet here is ideal: it looks like a made hand or a club and folds out a lot of unpaired hands, yet doesn’t commit my short stack. Plan: bet 300 into 600; fold to a large raise, and if called proceed cautiously on the turn (check/back off unless I improve or pick up strong info).

I have an open-ended straight draw (needing a 4 or a 9) on a 5-6-8 board. The pot is 900 and I need to call 300, giving me 3:1 pot odds. With 8 outs for the straight and a potential overcard (Queen), I have significant equity against most of Minni's range. Minni is extremely short-stacked with only 750 chips remaining after this bet. Calling is the best play here to realize my equity; if I hit my straight on the turn, I will easily be able to commit the rest of her chips. Even if I miss the turn, I may still be priced in to call a shove depending on the card and the pot odds.

I c‑bet the flop as a wide button steal and got called. The turn (9♦) does not improve J♣2♥ — I have no pair and no realistic draw (J♣ is only a blocker, there are only two clubs on board). Flash’s calling range is wide and includes many hands that now have at least a pair (9x, 6x, 8x, pocket pairs) or will continue with reasonable redraws. The pot (1,200) is already larger than my remaining stack; betting again would be high variance and effectively commit a big portion of my stack with very little equity. I’ll check to control the pot, avoid committing chips with air, and fold to any sizable turn shove/raise while evaluating a small stab only if the sizing is negligible.

I have completed a straight (5-6-7-8-9) on the turn with the 9 of diamonds. The pot currently stands at 1200 chips, and Minni, the small blind, has only 1050 chips remaining. Minni has checked to me. Given that the pot is already larger than her remaining stack, I should bet to put her all-in. This maximizes value from her potential sets, two pairs, or high pairs, and protects my hand against a possible flush draw (there are two clubs on the board). There is no reason to check and risk a free card for her or miss out on value from her short stack.

Stack/context: I’m Minni with J♣2♥, pot = 2,250 and it’s the turn (6♦ 8♣ 5♣ 9♦). I checked the turn to control the pot and was then raised to 1,050 — which is effectively an all‑in for my remaining 1,050. Calling would commit my entire stack. Equity and pot odds: calling 1,050 to win 3,300 (current pot 2,250 + my 1,050) requires ~31.8% equity. My realistic outs on the river are pairing the J or the 2 (3 jacks + 3 twos = 6 outs), ~6/46 ≈ 13% raw, and many of those outs are dead or don’t beat the opponent’s likely holdings. Opponent range and line: Flash called pre and on the flop, then sizes up on the turn — a line consistent with at least a pair (9x/8x/6x/5x), two pair/set, or a strong float/value hand. A bluff of this size from the big blind in this spot is possible but not common. Risk/Reward: I would be all‑in with essentially J‑high and only a ~13% chance to improve against a value range while needing ~32% to break even. That’s a clear folding spot. Decision: fold and preserve my remaining chips for a better spot.

I’m on the button with 6h8s and a short stack (1050 ≈ 10.5BB). Pot is 150 and it’s 100 to call. With ~10BB the correct strategy is push/fold — a small raise to 200 is too weak to earn reliable fold equity and calling leaves me in awkward postflop spots with very little fold equity remaining. 6‑8 offsuit is marginal postflop but as a button shove it has a lot of fold equity versus the blinds and simplifies decision-making. Flash (SB) is very deep/aggressive and Elon (BB) has ~58BB and will call sometimes, but the shove still maximizes expected value here. I’ll jam to pressure the blinds and either pick up the pot immediately or play for stacks if called.

I have been dealt pocket sevens (7d 7h) in the small blind. Minni, on the button, has made a very large open-raise to 1050 (more than 10x the big blind). This is a highly unusual bet sizing compared to previous hands. Pocket sevens are a strong hand, but with a short-stacked player in the big blind (Elon has only 5850) and a massive open from the button, I need to be cautious. However, sevens are too strong to fold pre-flop against a button open. I will call to see a flop, keeping the pot manageable for now, while being wary of Elon's potential shove from the big blind.

6c9d is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against a large button raise from Minni (1050 into 150, very aggressive) and a flat call from Flash in SB (loose but still indicates strength). Pot is 2200, calling 950 OOP as BB commits ~16% of my 5850 stack into a pot where I'll be out of position multiway with a speculative hand unlikely to realize equity. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities in this long 150-hand game. No strong read from history justifies hero-calling.

I am on the button with K4o. In a heads-up situation against Elon, who has a relatively short stack of 5,750 chips (57.5 big blinds), I should be raising most of my button range to put pressure on the blind. While K4o is not a premium hand, it is ahead of a random range and strong enough to open for a standard raise to 300 to take control of the pot early.

Preflop in BB facing a button minraise from Flash, who tends to raise wide from late position based on hand history (frequent button steals, folds to resistance sometimes). Hand is 3s7s: suited one-gapper with flush and straight potential, decent playability. Pot odds excellent: calling 200 into 400 (post-call pot 600, ~25% equity needed). Equity vs ~40% button range ~38-42%. Heads-up postflop I'll have position (SB acts first). Implied odds good with Flash's deep stack if I hit. Folding too tight; 3-betting risky as marginal bluff. Standard defend.

Flop completely misses my 3s7s: no pair, no straight draw, only backdoor spade flush draw with Js on board. Board Kc-8h-Js is coordinated (straight draw potential like Q-T, 9-T, 10-9) and hits Flash's wide button raising range (KJ+, KQ, AJs+, suited connectors). Preflop call was standard wide BB defense for playability, but now OOP after Flash checks (likely weak or trap). Pot 600 into my 5550 stack is manageable, but betting risks bloating pot OOP with air or getting check-raised off any equity. Checking controls pot, sees turn cheap, preserves stack for better spots, and induces bluffs on later streets if Flash has showdown value. Bluff betting thin here polarizes range poorly vs deep-stacked opponent who check-raises monsters. Standard check-back.

I have flopped Top Pair with Kings on a Jack-high board. Since I was the pre-flop aggressor and Elon has checked to me, I will place a continuation bet. This bet is for value, as I likely have the best hand against Elon's defending range, which includes Jacks, 8s, and some straight draws like T9. A half-pot bet of 300 chips is a standard size to build the pot while keeping his weaker calling range in.

Facing a small continuation bet of 300 into 900 on a Kc-8h-Js flop that completely misses my 3s7s (no pair, no made draw, only weak backdoor spade flush and negligible straight potential). Preflop call was fine for BB defense OOP, and check was correct to pot control. Flash's small bet sizing suggests a wide range including bluffs, weak pairs, and draws (e.g., QTo, T9s, suited Ax), but my hand has minimal equity (~15-25% at best even vs polarized range) and poor playability going forward OOP with deep stacks. Pot odds are ~3:1 (25% needed), but folding high-equity spots later or bluff-catching is better than realizing equity with air here. No fold equity if I raise, and calling bloats pot OOP for turns I'll often miss. Standard fold preserves stack for premium spots.

Preflop from SB (Button) heads-up effectively against Flash in BB. Hand is A6o, which is a strong playable hand in this spot with position postflop. Standard play is to raise for initiative, build the pot with ace blocker, and take advantage of wide SB opening range vs BB. Raising to 3x BB (300) is standard sizing. Opponent has deep stack, but I have good playability. Folding too tight, limping too passive.

I am in the Big Blind with T8o. Elon is on the Button/Small Blind and has a very short stack of 5250 chips (about 52.5 big blinds). He has raised to 300. T8o is a reasonable defending hand in a heads-up or blind-vs-blind situation, but given the current pot odds (200 to call for a 400 pot) and my position, I will call to see a flop. Folding would be too tight, and raising doesn't seem necessary with a medium-strength hand against a short stack who might be shoving soon.

On a paired board of 4h 4c 9s, I have checked my hand (Ten-high). As the Big Blind defending against a pre-flop raise, it is standard to check to the aggressor. This board is relatively dry, and while I haven't connected with it, Elon may also have missed. I will check to see how he reacts and potentially see a turn for free.

Heads-up on flop as preflop raiser from SB (OOP postflop). Board 4h4c9s is paired and dry, favoring my range which includes overpairs, Ax, sets, etc. Flash's check from BB after calling raise suggests weakness, likely floating or weak pairs/draws. Ah provides blocker to top pair and ace-high showdown value. Ace-high has decent equity (~40-50% vs calling range). C-bet 2/3 pot (400) for value/thin value from Ax/9x worse, fold equity on paired board where Flash unlikely to have two pair or better often. Protects against villainous floats. Stack sizes allow it (my 5250 effective), pot control not necessary with initiative. Checking risks giving free card to overcards/draws.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-high on a board of 4-4-9. Elon, who is playing a relatively short stack of about 50 big blinds, raised pre-flop and has now fired a continuation bet of 400 into a 600 pot. While the board is dry and Elon could be bluffing with overcards, T8o has very little equity here. I have no draws and only two overcards that might not even be clean outs if they hit. Given Elon's stack size and the lack of potential for my hand to improve to a winner, I will fold and wait for a better spot rather than floating with a weak hand.

I am on the Button in a heads-up situation against Elon. Ten-Eight offsuit is a standard opening hand in this position. Since I have a significant chip lead and Elon has a relatively short stack (around 57 big blinds), I will put in a standard 3x raise to 300 to take control of the pot early.

AQo is a premium hand heads-up against a button raise from Flash, who appears loose-aggressive based on hand history with frequent raises and continuation bets. Button ranges are wide, giving AQo excellent equity (~45-50% vs typical open range) and domination potential. Out of position, but 3-betting takes initiative, builds pot with value, denies equity to weaker aces/off-suit broadways, and has good fold equity vs light opens. Calling OOP risks playing post-flop awkwardly against floats/c-bets. Raising to 900 (3x his raise) is standard sizing: pot-committing neither too shallow (min 600 too small) nor too deep given stacks (my 5750 effective). Fold too tight pre. Maximizes EV over session.

I am heads-up against Elon, who has a relatively short stack of 4950 chips. My 10-8 offsuit is a marginal hand that plays poorly against a 3-bet from the big blind. Elon's raise to 900 represents strength, and calling would put me in a difficult position post-flop out of position with a weak hand. Given the stack depths and the strength shown, folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my chip lead.

AsTs is a premium heads-up hand from the SB/Button position: suited Ace with ten kicker, excellent playability, straight potential, flush draw potential, and top pair top kicker potential. With position postflop and a healthy 61BB stack, raising standardly builds the pot or takes down blinds uncontested. Opponent Flash has a deep stack but we've been heads-up recently. Standard raise to 3x BB (300 total).

I have Ace-King suited, which is a premium hand. Elon has a relatively short stack of about 58.5 big blinds. I should 3-bet to build the pot and potentially get the money in against his range, which he has shown to play aggressively from the button.

Facing a 3-bet from Flash to 1000 after my open to 300, with AsTs on the button vs BB. AsTs has excellent equity against typical 3-bet ranges (dominated by worse Ax, suited broadways, pairs), strong suited playability, position advantage, flush and straight potential. Pot odds are favorable: calling 700 into 1300 (pot will be 2000), and postflop position allows realizing equity effectively. 4-betting risks bloating pot OOP response or folding equity not necessary with this hand's strength; folding is too tight preflop in position. Flash deep-stacked but my 58BB stack supports calling. Standard play is to call and play postflop.

On this flop, I have two overcards and a backdoor straight draw. Elon has about 5,000 chips left. As the pre-flop aggressor in a 3-bet pot, I should continue with a bet to put pressure on his marginal hands and protect my equity. A bet of around half the pot is standard here to set up a potential shove on later streets if I improve or to simply take the pot down now.

On the flop of 2s Qs Td, holding As Ts, I have middle pair (Tens) with the nut spade flush draw (9 outs), giving me massive equity (~35-50% against most of Flash's continuing range). Position on the button allows me to realize my equity well. Flash's 3-bet preflop and half-pot c-bet suggest a strong range like QQ+, AK, QJ, but he can have worse Qx, Ax suited, or draws. Raising extracts value from weaker pairs/draws, charges opponent draws, applies fold equity against his bluffs/air, and protects my hand. Calling keeps the pot smaller but allows him to draw cheaply; shoving is overplay with 5150 stack into 3000 pot as he has 31k and can call lighter. Raising to 3k (3x his bet) is balanced size: builds pot when ahead, gets value, and leaves fold equity if he has marginal hands. Pot will be 6000 if called, with position for turn.

I have Ace-King clubs on a board of Qs-Td-2s. Pre-flop, I 3-bet to 1000 and Elon called. On the flop, I led for 1000 and Elon raised to 3000. However, Elon's total remaining stack at the start of the flop action was only 4850. By raising to 3000, he only has 1850 chips remaining behind. The pot is currently 6000 and it costs me 2000 to call. Given that Elon is essentially pot-committed and I have two overcards and a backdoor straight draw, I am committed to the hand. Since he only has 1850 left, I will raise to put him all-in.

Facing Flash's flop 3-bet to 5000 after my raise to 3000 on a 2s Qs Td board with As Ts (middle pair Tens + nut spade flush draw). Equity is exceptional: ~35% vs sets like QQ (pure FD), 45-55% vs Qx/top pair (dominate + FD outs), 60%+ vs AK/AQ spades or overpairs/draws. Board draw-heavy (flush/straight draws possible), hits his 3-bet pre range (QQ+, QJs, AKs) but I crush worse Qx/AKs. Pot 10000, call 2000 into ~12000 (14% needed), but shove better. Remaining stack 2150 allows exact min-raise all-in to 5150 total (add 2150 more), forcing him to call 650 more into 12150 pot. This extracts max value from worse (Qx, AJs spades, overpairs fearing sets), applies pressure on marginals/draws he might fold to raise but call flat, denies turn equity, and commits us fully with SPR ~0 now vs awkward 150-behind post-call. History shows Flash aggressive but folds to heat sometimes (e.g., Hand 27, 32). No fold equity needed but sizing perfect. Folding absurd with this strength.

Elon has moved all-in for his remaining 5150 chips. Having already committed 5000 chips to the pot, I only need to call 150 more to compete for a total pot of 12150. Even though I only have Ace-high, the pot odds are overwhelming (over 80-to-1). I have two overcards and a backdoor straight draw, which gives me enough equity to make this call mandatory regardless of what Elon is holding.
